title: Virtual fullloop — satellite panel sequence & candidate preflight
preview:
From: Kimberly Park <kimberly@redwood.com>
To: Alina Novak <alina.novak@gmail.com>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 11 Jun 2029 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Virtual fullloop — propose multi-slot sequence and preflight

Hi Alina —

Great to hear you can move forward with the fullloop. We want to make the day efficient and leave a 30-minute debrief for panelists afterward. Below are a couple of options — please pick any that work for you, or flag constraints and we’ll adapt: 

Option A (preferred):
- 09:30–10:00 — Culture & IC fit (Laura Bennett)
- 10:10–10:40 — System design (Karthik Iyer)
- 11:00–11:30 — Product + cross-functional (Marissa Cole + Logan Wright)
- 11:40–12:10 — Exec / leadership convo (Kimberly Park)
- 12:15–12:45 — Panel debrief (internal only)

Option B (if you need a later start):
- 12:30–13:00 — Culture & IC fit
- 13:15–13:45 — System design
- 14:00–14:30 — Product + cross-functional
- 14:45–15:15 — Exec convo
- 15:20–15:50 — Panel debrief

Prep + attachments: I’ve attached a short interview guide and a checklist (resume attached). Please reply with your preferred option and any accessibility or scheduling notes. We’ll send formal calendar invites once panelists confirm.

Thanks — excited to meet you.

Best,
Kimberly Park
Hiring Manager, Platform
Redwood Inference

Attachments: Alina_Novak_Resume.pdf (pdf), Redwood_FullLoop_InterviewGuide.pdf (pdf)


From: Alina Novak <alina.novak@gmail.com>
To: Kimberly Park <kimberly@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 11 Jun 2029 12:01:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Virtual fullloop — propose multi-slot sequence and preflight

Hi Kimberly —

Thank you — I can do Option A on Thursday (09:30 start). One small ask: can the Product panel be split across two people instead of both joining the same 30-minute block? I find it easier when each interviewer has a focused 20-minute slot. Also I prefer Google Meet links and a short tech check 10 minutes prior if possible.

Looking forward to it —
Alina

--
Alina Novak
Senior Software Engineer
alina.novak@gmail.com
+1 (415) 555-0147

- 09:30–10:00 — Culture & IC fit (Laura)
- 10:10–10:40 — System design (Karthik)
- 11:00–11:20 — Product (Marissa)
- 11:25–11:45 — Product (Logan)
- 11:55–12:25 — Exec / leadership convo (Kimberly)
- 12:30–13:00 — Panel debrief (internal only)

Also tagging logistical items: tech check 09:20–09:30 (Kimberly will host), provide candidate prereads (attached), and I’ll issue calendar invites with meeting links once we have confirmations.

Thanks —
Kimberly

> Forwarded attachments: Redwood_FullLoop_InterviewGuide.pdf, candidate-preflight-checklist.pdf

To: Kimberly Park <kimberly@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>, Logan Wright <logan@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 11 Jun 2029 13:42:00 -07:00

Date: Wed, 12 Jun 2029 08:16:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Fwd: Virtual fullloop — Alina confirms Option A + small tweak (split product panel)

I’m available for a 30-minute system design session at 10:10–10:40. I’ll reserve 10:05–10:10 for context switching.

Karthik

To: Alina Novak <alina.novak@gmail.com>

Subject: Final schedule + day-of logistics (tech check + calendar invites)

Hi Alina —

Thanks again for confirming. Finalized schedule (Google Meet links attached in invites):

- 09:20–09:30 — Tech check (Kimberly) — Meet: https://meet.redwood.com/kimberly/techcheck
- 09:30–10:00 — Culture & IC fit (Laura Bennett)
- 10:10–10:40 — System design (Karthik Iyer)
- 11:00–11:20 — Product (Marissa Cole)
- 11:25–11:45 — Product (Logan Wright)
- 11:55–12:25 — Exec / leadership convo (Kimberly Park)

Please join the tech check 10 minutes early so we can confirm audio/video and your screen share settings. If anything changes, reply to this thread and we’ll adapt.

Best of luck — we’re excited to learn more about your work.

Cheers,

title: Scheduling: Virtual onsite for Elliott Harris — full loop sequencing
preview:
From: Jenny Marks <jenny.marks@talentworks.co>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 10 Apr 2029 10:12:00 -0700
Subject: Elliott Harris — candidate availability + resume

Hi team,

Attaching Elliott's resume (Elliott_Harris_resume.pdf). He's excited about the role and is available for a virtual full loop next week. He is located in Austin (CT) and prefers morning slots. Below are the times he proposed (all CT):

- Tue 2029-04-16: 9:00–12:00 CT (flexible)
- Wed 2029-04-17: 11:00–15:00 CT (less preferred)
- Thu 2029-04-18: 8:30–11:30 CT (best)

High-level ask: can Redwood run a condensed 4–5 hour virtual onsite with a short debrief window immediately after? If so, what panel sequencing do you prefer (deep-dive first or system-design first)?

I copied Elliott's contact below for direct calendar invite if we confirm a slot.

— Jenny
Jenny Marks
Senior Recruiter, TalentWorks
jenny.marks@talentworks.co
+1 415-555-0129

Attachments: Elliott_Harris_resume.pdf


From: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.ai>
To: Jenny Marks <jenny.marks@talentworks.co>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 11 Apr 2029 09:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Elliott Harris — candidate availability + resume

Thanks, Jenny — resume received and looks good.

We can run a condensed full loop the week of the 16th. My preference for sequencing given a 4.5 hour window:

1) 30 min intro + team sync (Logan)
2) 60 min system design (Senior SWE)
3) 45 min product/PM cross-functional (Laura)
4) 45 min deep dive: infra/runtime or algorithm (Marissa)
5) 30 min culture + manager interview (Hiring mgr)
6) 30 min buffer + 30 min debrief (internal)

Notes:
- We try to keep the longest blocks earlier to avoid cognitive drift.
- Please preserve a 30–45 minute debrief slot immediately after so interviewers can capture notes while fresh.

I've attached a draft .ics for Thu 2029-04-18 08:30–13:00 CT to test calendar conversions (redwood_virtual_onsite_draft.ics). If Thu is best for Elliott, we'll finalize times and assign interviewer names to each block.

Kimberly — can you confirm Senior SWE availability for the system-design block? Laura/Marissa, please flag any conflicts for Thursday morning.

Thanks,
Logan
Logan Wright
Recruiting Partner (Interviews), Redwood Inference
logan.wright@redwood.ai


From: Elliott Harris <elliott.harris+rf@gmail.com>
To: Jenny Marks <jenny.marks@talentworks.co>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.ai>
Date: Fri, 12 Apr 2029 11:20:00 -05:00
Subject: Re: Elliott Harris — candidate availability + resume

Hi Jenny / Logan,

Thanks for routing. Thu 4/18 is my preferred day — the Thu window works, and I can do 08:30 CT start. If you need me earlier or later I can flex by 30 minutes. I can join by Google Meet; link will be the one you put on the invite.

A couple of favors:
- Can interviewers share a 1–2 line prep note for each block? Helps me focus my answers.
- Please add the hiring manager's name so I can prepare a couple of questions.

Looking forward to it.

— Elliott
Elliott Harris
Senior Software Engineer (Candidate)
elliott.harris+rf@gmail.com


From: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.ai>, Jenny Marks <jenny.marks@talentworks.co>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.ai>
Date: Sun, 14 Apr 2029 08:47:00 -0700

Confirmed on our side for Thu 4/18 08:30–13:00 CT. Proposed interviewer assignments (all times CT):

08:30–09:00 — Intro + hiring manager quick sync — Logan Wright (Logan) / Priya N. (Manager)
09:00–10:00 — System design — Marcus Lin (Senior SWE)
10:00–10:15 — Break
10:15–11:00 — Product + PM partnership — Laura Bennett (Product)
11:00–11:45 — Infra/runtime deep dive — Marissa Cole (Infra)
11:45–12:15 — Culture / Manager — Priya N. (Hiring mgr)
12:15–12:45 — Internal debrief (calendared for interviewers only)

A few operational items:
- Marcus cannot start earlier than 09:00; confirmed.
- Please set all invites with a shared doc link for the scorecard (panel-scorecard-template.docx) and include the Google Meet link.
- Marissa, will you own the infra prep bullet points? Logan, can you add Priya's pronouns and title in the invite (Priya Natarajan, Eng Manager)?

Jenny / Elliott: I'll add a 1–2 line prep blurb in each invite block tomorrow morning and upload the scorecard.

Laura

title: Guest circulation map, brochure crate routing, and greeter placement — CloudExpo West (Booth A3)
preview:
From: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>
To: Expo Logistics <ops@expo-logistics.com>, CloudExpo Sponsorship <sponsorship@cloudexpo.com>
Cc: Naomi Feldman <naomi@redwood.com>, Logan Wright <logan@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
Date: 2028-10-19T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: Guest circulation map, brochure crate routing, and greeter placement — CloudExpo West (Booth A3)

Hi team,

We need to lock down the guest circulation for Booth A3 and confirm where the brochure crates should be staged for load-in. High-level asks: 
- Greeter station placement (we want a staffed greeter to capture badges and hand out the overview brochure)
- Two brochure crate drop locations (one for day 1 morning replenishment, one for on-site storage)
- A short guest-flow sketch we can hand to the AV/rigging crew so demo lanes don't collide with the main aisle

Expo Logistics, are there any hard constraints for A3 (no-go zones, column sightlines) we should be aware of? Naomi/Logan — I need estimated brochure counts and whether we can split crates to the back-of-house during off-peak hours.

Attaching initial floorplan for reference — I'm working on a quick hand sketch and will share tomorrow.

Thanks,
Karthik
Product Events Lead
Redwood Inference

Attachments: exhibit_floorplan_v3.pdf (pdf)


From: Expo Logistics <ops@expo-logistics.com>
To: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>
Cc: CloudExpo Sponsorship <sponsorship@cloudexpo.com>, Naomi Feldman <naomi@redwood.com>, Logan Wright <logan@redwood.com>
Date: 2028-10-19T11:47:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Guest circulation map, brochure crate routing, and greeter placement — CloudExpo West (Booth A3)

Hi Karthik,

Thanks — floorplan constraints for A3:
- Column 4 (north end) creates a 5 ft sightline setback; any tall verticals there will need a 1.5 ft offset.
- House aisle to the west is 12 ft; main flow will be along that edge.
- Power chase runs along the back wall, which limits crate staging behind the counters.

Proposal: Greeter at the northeast corner of A3 (adjacent to house aisle) to capture traffic without blocking demo lanes. Staging: one crate at the rear-left behind the demo counters (back-of-house access), and one crate at the loading-dock-access side for morning replenishment. We can route replenishment during the first 30 minutes of each show day to avoid peak congestion.

I attached the latest plan with proposed placement overlays. If the greeter needs a small 2x2 table + banner, confirm and we will add it to the rigging manifest.

Best,
Ana Rivera
Ops Lead, Expo Logistics

Attachments: exhibit_floorplan_v3_markup.pdf (pdf)


From: Naomi Feldman <naomi@redwood.com>
To: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>, Expo Logistics <ops@expo-logistics.com>
Cc: Logan Wright <logan@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>, PrintPro Signs <orders@printpro.com>
Date: 2028-10-20T08:33:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Guest circulation map, brochure crate routing, and greeter placement — CloudExpo West (Booth A3)

Quick numbers:
- Expected daily attendees through A3 (estimate based on last year): 850–1,100.
- Brochure type A (4pp overview): plan 1,200/day. We want 1,500 day-1 to cover giveaways and booth staff needs.
- Brochure type B (one-sheet product summary): 600/day.

Logistics ask: can crates be split into three boxes (2x 600 + 1x 300) so we can stage a smaller replenishment box at the greeter table instead of moving the full crate? PrintPro, can you confirm dimension/weight per box and if there's a nominal pallet option?

Also: we need one vertical billboard (60\"x30\") on a retractable stand at the greeter so the sponsor logo is visible for photos. PrintPro, cc'd, please send production turnaround and a quote.

Naomi
Marketing Ops
Redwood Inference


From: PrintPro Signs <orders@printpro.com>
To: Naomi Feldman <naomi@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>
Cc: Expo Logistics <ops@expo-logistics.com>
Date: 2028-10-20T14:20:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Quote for vertical billboard and crate packaging options

Hi Naomi/Karthik — thanks for the details.

Estimate summary (lead time 5 business days from approved artwork):
- 60\"x30\" retractable banner (single-sided, 13oz vinyl, matte): $185 each (includes stand).
- Split crate packaging: we can produce 3 boxes sized to your counts. Weight per 600-brochure box ~ 22 kg; 300 box ~ 11 kg. Boxes are standard export-grade corrugated with printed labels.
- Pallet option (if you want a single pallet for forklift movement): $55/pallet + shrinkwrap.

Attached: detailed quote and a mockup for the retractable banner. If you approve, please send print-ready artwork or a Google Drive link and we will schedule production for 10/24 pickup.

Regards,
Imran Khan
PrintPro Signs

Attachments: quote_vprintpro_20281020.pdf (pdf)


From: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>
To: Naomi Feldman <naomi@redwood.com>, Logan Wright <logan@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>, PrintPro Signs <orders@printpro.com>, Expo Logistics <ops@expo-logistics.com>
Date: 2028-10-22T14:05:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Guest circulation map, brochure crate routing, and greeter placement — CloudExpo West (Booth A3)

Thanks everyone — final decisions below: 
- Greeter: northeast corner as proposed. Provide small 2x2 table and a single retractable banner (60x30). PrintPro: proceed with the quoted banner (Imran, PJ) — I'll drop the final artwork in the Drive link by EOD 10/23.
- Crate routing: split into three boxes (600, 600, 300). Morning replenishment box to be staged at loading-dock-access side; back-of-house box behind demo counters. Expo Logistics to include replenishment window 8:30–9:00am daily on the rigging manifest.
- Pallet: no pallet — we want hand-carryable boxes to move quickly on the floor.

Attaching final manifest and a quick guest flow sketch for AV/rigging. Please confirm receipt.

Karthik
Product Events Lead
Redwood Inference

Attachments: final_crate_manifest.csv (csv), guest_flow_sketch.png (png)

preview:
From: Kimberly Park <kimberly@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>, Logan Wright <logan@redwood.com>, Sana Patel <sana@clearpathhealth.com>, Jamie O'Neill <jamie.oneill@medai.com>
Cc:

Subject: Post-event lead bridge: ClearPath webinar — replay & MQL routing

Thanks team — quick post-show dispatch. Attached is the raw attendee export from the ClearPath Health webinar (attendee_export_0809_raw.csv). TL;DR: we have 412 attendees, ~78 MQL-eligible by score, and 23 accounts that match our high-touch ICP.

Action items (please follow in the order below):
- SDR claim window: 48 hours. If you plan to pursue, add your name to the Claimed_By column on the cleaned sheet (Marissa will upload).
- Priority definition: MQL >= 70 OR company in ICP list flagged as HighTouch. HighTouch accounts should be routed to the enterprise queue directly and tagged for AE engagement.
- Replay & assets: public replay link (access controlled) is in the attached replay_access_link.txt and in the Google Drive. Use the \"On-Demand Demo\" CTA for Warm leads.
- Outreach cadence suggestion: 1) Warm email w/ replay + case study (day 1), 2) Sales touch (call + LinkedIn connect by day 3), 3) Demo offer (by day 7).

High-touch / immediate to claim (top 23 — first-come basis):
- ClearPath Health (Sana Patel) — enterprise/PHR integrator
- MedAI Solutions (Jamie O'Neill) — evaling inference cost/latency
- Broadwell Labs — research partnership interest
(Full list attached; see cleaned sheet for duplicates removed.)

Please confirm by 11am PT that you see the sheet and which accounts you plan to claim. If anything looks off — duplicates, partner leads, or a compliance flag — flag it in-thread.

Attachments: attendee_export_0809_raw.csv, replay_access_link.txt

Thanks,
Kimberly Park
Head of Field Marketing
Redwood Inference

title: Deal metadata scrub — Amount / SKU / Next steps (please action)
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
To: Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>, Logan Wright <logan@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 23 May 2028 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Deal metadata scrub — Amount / SKU / Next steps (please action)

Team,

Quick heads-up before the morning forecast readout: Finance flagged a handful of mid‑to‑late stage deals where amount, product (SKU), and next‑step fields are inconsistent with our most recent contract or PO. This is driving noise in the commit column and causing a couple of false positives in projected ARR for the week.

Target: please validate the deals assigned to you in the HubSpot view (link below) by EOD today. Minimal required updates:
- Confirm contract amount matches the latest PO (Amount field)
- Ensure Product/SKU is one of the canonical values (use the Q3 lookup in the attached template)
- Set an explicit Next Step and a one‑sentence plan in the \"Next Step\" field
- Reassess Probability: update if new information changes the odds by ±10% or more

I attached a small CSV template to make this quick (Q3-scrub-template.csv). If you run into a difference that needs a legal/finance check (billing mismatch, upgraded SKU, or revenue timing change), flag me and cc finance.

HubSpot view: https://app.hubspot.com/deal/123456789 (filter: Q3 open > $50k)

Thanks — small asks, big upside for forecast credibility.

Ben
Ben Carter
Director, Sales Operations
Redwood Inference

Attachments: Q3-scrub-template.csv (spreadsheet/csv)

- Probability: current HS probability 80% — given the billing cadence shift and legal amend, I'll move this to 65% until amendment is signed.

title: Urgent: convert onsite to hybrid for today's interview (Arjun Mehta)
preview:
From: Arjun Mehta <arjun.mehta+applicant@gmail.com>
To: Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwood.ai>
Cc: recruiter@talentscout.co
Date: 2028-05-17T07:15:00-07:00
Subject: Can't make onsite this morning — ambulance, can we do hybrid?

Hi Irene,

I'm really sorry for the short notice — on my way to the office I was in a minor collision and ended up in an ambulance for observation. My doctor cleared me but advised I avoid long travel today. I can still interview remotely (phone/video) and finish the coding exercise at home if that's possible.

My original onsite start was 10:30am PT. I can be available by phone at 11:15 or 2:30pm, or I can do a condensed 60–75 minute remote session today if any panelists can accommodate.

I've attached a quick photo of the discharge note in case you need it.

Really appreciate any flexibility — I want to keep things moving if the panel prefers to meet today.

Thanks,
Arjun Mehta
(415) 555-0198
Attachments: doctor_note.jpg, resume.pdf


From: Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwood.ai>
To: Arjun Mehta <arjun.mehta+applicant@gmail.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.ai>, Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwood.ai>, recruiter@talentscout.co
Date: 2028-05-17T08:05:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Can't make onsite this morning — ambulance, can we do hybrid?

Arjun — thanks for the quick update and glad to hear you were cleared. No worries about the short notice.

Logan / Grace / Marissa — quick ask: our 10:30 onsite with Arjun is today. He can't travel but can do remote. Two options that would keep the interview on the same day:

- Option A (preferred if panel agrees): Convert to a hybrid same-day plan — 45 minutes video panel (technical + design discussion) + 45 minutes take-home coding task that Arjun would complete after the call and upload within 24 hours.

title: Environment bridgework plan & signoff for evaluation
preview:
From: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.ai>
To: Julia Park <julia@medscale.com>
Cc: Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.ai>, Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 09 May 2028 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Environment bridgework plan & signoff for evaluation

Hi Julia —

Thanks again for the sync this morning. Per our conversation I drafted a concise environment bridgework plan that maps the evaluation milestones to the infra tasks we discussed (API keys, org setup, IP allowlist, VPC peering). The goal is to get a minimal production-like evaluation environment up within two full weeks so your engineers can run end-to-end load tests by week three.

Attached: env-bridge-checklist.xlsx (single-sheet checklist) and medscale-map-draft-v2.docx (MAP with responsibilities). High-level items:
- Week 0 (today): confirm org admin and contact for API keys issuance
- Day 1–3: org setup + API key creation and scoping (dev vs test keys)
- Day 4–7: IP allowlist + firewall rule verification (we'll provide expected egress IPs)
- Day 6–10: VPC peering + route validation (small NAT/proxy test)
- Day 11–14: smoke tests + token rotation dry-run + signoff

Immediate asks from your side: please confirm the org admin name and whether you want Redwood to manage a single test API key or two scoped keys (dev/test). If you prefer two keys, note any IP restrictions per key.

Rishi and Grace are looped for the peering and security items — Rishi will sketch the peering NAT/routing notes; Grace will own the smoke test criteria and acceptance checklist.

Thanks — happy to jump on a 30m call if you want to walk through the checklist.

Best,
Logan Wright
Senior Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference
[Attachment: env-bridge-checklist.xlsx (application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.spreadsheetml.sheet)]
[Attachment: medscale-map-draft-v2.docx (application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.wordprocessingml.document)]

From: Julia Park <julia@medscale.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.ai>
Cc: MedScale Ops <ops@medscale.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.ai>, Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 09 May 2028 14:03:00 -0400
Subject: Re: Environment bridgework plan & signoff for evaluation

Logan —

Appreciate the clear plan. A couple quick clarifications before we commit to the timeline:
1) Org admin: that will be me (julia@medscale.com) but our infra lead (ops@medscale.com) will own peering/VPC tasks.
2) API keys: we'd prefer two keys (dev/test) with separate scopes. Can you confirm what default rate limits you apply to test keys?
3) IP allowlist: we need the exact egress IPs and a CIDR preference — do you expect us to open /32 entries or a small /28?
4) Peering: can you confirm whether Redwood requires cross-account role access or mutual peer acceptance? Our security team prefers not to grant cross-account roles.

We can do the signoff call Friday morning (ET) but need answers to 2–4 to prep our infra team.

Thanks,
Julia Park
Head of Product Ops | MedScale
[MedScale Ops included for peering details]

From: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.ai>
To: Julia Park <julia@medscale.com>
Cc: Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.ai>, Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 10 May 2028 08:45:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Environment bridgework plan & signoff for evaluation

Julia — thanks, answers inline below and updated timeline notes. Copying the sections into the thread so your infra can forward to ops.

2) API keys and rate limits:
- We'll provision two keys (dev/test). Default test key limits: 600 requests/minute and soft token cap of 200k tokens/day; dev key will be throttled lower by default (200 req/min). If you expect sustained load for the eval, we can raise the soft caps on a per-deal basis; just flag expected QPS and max sequence length.

3) IP allowlist:
- We provide a small pool of egress IPs. For the initial peer we recommend adding four /32 entries (we'll supply the list) to minimize blast radius. If you'd rather use a /28 later we can move to that in Week 2.

4) VPC peering and access model:
- For security alignment we support two patterns: (A) mutual VPC peering (your account <> Redwood account) with no cross-account roles required, or (B) customer-managed cloud connector (you maintain a NAT/proxy that reaches our egress IPs). Pattern A is simplest for low-latency tests; Pattern B is fallback if granting peering is blocked. We'll default to Pattern A unless you tell us otherwise.

Updated timeline (adjusted for two keys + initial peering):
- Day 0 (today): Julia confirms org admin (confirmed) and ops contact (ops@medscale.com)
- Day 1: Redwood issues dev/test keys (scoped) and shares raw token and rotation plan via secure link
- Day 2–4: IP allowlist entries exchanged and applied (we'll provide four /32s)
- Day 3–7: Establish VPC peering (mutual) + route validation; quick connectivity smoke test
- Day 8–12: Load smoke tests (we provide test harness) + token rotation dry-run
- Day 13–14: Signoff meeting and shared acceptance doc signed

I've attached a filled checklist with placeholder IPs and a DOCX MAP with assigned owners. If this looks good I'll create the DocuSign signoff and share it with you + ops for signature.

Best,
Logan
Senior Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference
[Attachment: env-bridge-checklist.xlsx (updated)]
[Attachment: medscale-map-draft-v2.docx (updated)]

From: Julia Park <julia@medscale.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.ai>
Cc: Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.ai>, Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwood.ai>, MedScale Ops <ops@medscale.com>
Date: Sat, 11 May 2028 11:27:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Environment bridgework plan & signoff for evaluation

Logan —

This looks good and we prefer Pattern A (mutual VPC peering). Ops will accept the peer request and apply the four /32 IP entries for the initial test window. We'll also confirm expected QPS for the load test by EOD Monday.

Please go ahead and create the DocuSign for the MAP signoff and share the link to both me and ops@medscale.com. Once the DocuSign is complete we'll schedule the Friday morning signoff call (ET) as discussed.

Thank you — excited to get this running.

Julia Park
Head of Product Ops | MedScale
[Attachment: docusign-map-signoff.pdf (application/pdf)]

title: Cross-entity replay charges — request for split allocation, VAT treatment, and corrected invoice
preview:
From: Elena Rossi <accounts-payable@acmerobotics.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>
Cc: Acme Robotics Finance <finance@acmerobotics.com>
Date: Thu, 12 Apr 2028 09:17:00 -0400
Subject: Invoice INV-20280412 hold — large embed replay charges and VAT classification

Hi Logan,

We received invoice INV-20280412 and have placed payment on hold. On line items 4-6 there are ~1.2M embed-replay units billed to Acme Robotics that our procurement team does not recognize — the usage appears to have originated from a different Acme entity (APAC subsidiary) and should not be charged to our EU entity. Separately, the VAT line shows 20% applied to the full amount; because the traffic was cross-border and belongs to an APAC buyer, we believe EU reverse-charge should apply (no VAT on the invoice).

Can you provide: 1) the raw usage export CSV for 2028-04-12 through 2028-04-15 with prefix and entity tags; 2) an explanation of why the replay traffic was attributed to Acme Robotics EU; 3) timeline for a corrected invoice or credit note so we can release the payment?

Attachments: INV-20280412-acme.pdf (invoice), usage_export_20280412.csv (sample from AP events)

Thanks — we need this resolved for our month-end.

Best,
Elena
Accounts Payable
Acme Robotics


From: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>
To: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: 
Date: Thu, 12 Apr 2028 10:04:00 -0400
Subject: Fwd: Invoice INV-20280412 hold — large embed replay charges and VAT classification

Kimberly / Ben — forwarding a hold from Acme AP regarding a ~1.2M embed-replay charge they claim belongs to a different Acme entity (APAC). They also dispute VAT. Can you do a quick allocation + VAT triage?

Quick notes: I pulled the usage export link and attached the invoice they sent. On first pass I see a high concentration of requests with prefix \"acme-apac-ingest\" that were processed under Acme's EU billing tag; this looks like a routing/tenant-tagging issue during a temporary migration window.

What I need from you for reply to Elena:
- Confirm whether we can reattribute those units to Acme APAC (give exact unit counts)
- Propose a credit note or corrected invoice (legal/tax input may be needed)
- ETA for corrected invoicing so they can release payment (they flagged month-end)

If you need me to loop in Tax/Legal please say and I will escalate.

Thanks,
Logan
Senior Revenue Operations
Redwood Inference


From: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: 
Date: Fri, 13 Apr 2028 08:21:00 -0400
Subject: Re: Fwd: Invoice INV-20280412 hold — large embed replay charges and VAT classification

Thanks Logan — I pulled the full usage export and did a short audit. Summary:
- Count: 1,204,380 embed-replay units between 2028-04-12T02:00Z and 2028-04-15T18:00Z.
- Attribution: 98% of those events have tenant_prefix=acme-apac-ingest and origin_region=ap-southeast-1, but our billing ingestion used the EU billing_tag (acme-eu-prod) for that window due to a short-lived tenant-tag sync failure during the deployment on 2028-04-12.
- Root cause: a config roll that reset tenant-tag mappings in the router; affected requests were routed to an EU routing pool that stamped the EU billing tag.

Proposal:
1) Reallocate 1,204,380 embed-replay units from Acme EU to Acme APAC in our billing ledger (apply to their APAC account).
2) Issue a corrected invoice to Acme EU removing those lines and a credit note for the overcharge (proposed credit attached).
3) For VAT: recommend treating the reallocated traffic as supplied to an APAC entity — issue credit without EU VAT and annotate reverse-charge where appropriate. We should loop in Tax for formal sign-off; I can draft a one-paragraph rationale for their records.

Attachments: proposed_credit_note_ACME-8372.pdf, allocation_evidence_20280412.csv

If we can get Tax sign-off today I can generate the credit note and request Finance to reissue a corrected invoice by COB Monday. Ben — can you confirm Finance is OK with an expedited credit and whether we should hold any penalty waivers?

Kimberly
Billing & Revenue Ops
Redwood Inference


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>
Cc: 
Date: Fri, 13 Apr 2028 09:02:00 -0400
Subject: Re: Fwd: Invoice INV-20280412 hold — large embed replay charges and VAT classification

Appreciate the audit, Kim. From Finance side:
- We can reallocate and issue a credit note for the EU invoice; no problem expediting.
- For VAT/legal: please get Tax/Legal to confirm reverse-charge treatment before we remove the VAT line. If Tax signs off, we'll withdraw the VAT amount and reissue invoice as net.
- Can you put the allocation evidence and a one-paragraph signoff in the HubSpot deal record (ACME-8372) so AP teams have an auditable trail?

I'll notify AR to expect the corrected invoice and to release payment hold once the credit note is issued. ETA: we can have a draft credit note and corrected invoice Monday COB (2028-04-16) if Tax signs off today.

Ben
Director, Customer Finance
Redwood Inference


From: Elena Rossi <accounts-payable@acmerobotics.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>
Cc: Acme Robotics Finance <finance@acmerobotics.com>
Date: Tue, 25 Apr 2028 16:40:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Invoice INV-20280412 hold — large embed replay charges and VAT classification

Logan / Kimberly / Ben —

Thanks for the detailed audit and proposed credit note. We reviewed the allocation evidence and confirm those units should sit with our APAC entity. We need two items to close our hold and proceed with payment:
1) Corrected invoice (removed lines + VAT adjusted) showing net amount and reference to credit note ACME-CR-8372.
2) Short statement (1 paragraph) from Redwood Tax/Legal confirming reverse-charge treatment or that no EU VAT applies for the reallocated units. This will be passed to our tax team for their records.

If you can provide both by EOD Wednesday (2028-04-26) we will release the payment hold and remit the outstanding net balance. Please send corrected invoice PDF and the Tax/Legal statement to accounts-payable@acmerobotics.com.

Elena

title: Pack sculpting & stepdown rules — clarification and proration options
preview:
Date: Thu, 29 Mar 2028 09:12:00 -0700
From: Seth Romero <seth@mindwell.io>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>
Cc: Priya Shah <priya.shah@mindwell.io>
Subject: Question on packaging — stepdown rules & proration

Hi Ben / Kimberly,

Thanks again for the walkthrough yesterday. Priya and I dug into the PDF and spreadsheet you shared, and a couple of items need clarification before we lock the pricing: 

1) Stepdown rules: Our finance team wants to understand exactly how credits work if usage falls mid-quarter. Is there an automatic prorate to token credits, or do customers move down a tier on the next invoice cycle?

2) Feature gating / bundled features: We rely on the "priority routing" feature for latency-sensitive endpoints. If we downgrade one tier, does that feature stay on for a short grace window or is it toggled off immediately?

3) Discount ask: For a two-year commitment we were hoping for a modest stepdown protection credit (to cover up to 20% downward usage variance). Is that something Redwood can include in the MA?

Attaching our initial redlines is easier for context — can you confirm how those would interact with the packaging rules?

Thanks,
Seth

--
Seth Romero
Head of Platform Partnerships
MindWell Health
seth@mindwell.io
+1 415-555-0123

Date: Thu, 29 Mar 2028 10:05:00 -0700
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Seth Romero <seth@mindwell.io>
Cc: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>
Subject: Re: Question on packaging — stepdown rules & proration

Hi Seth — thanks for the clear list. Short answers up top, details below.

- Stepdown/proration: We do not automatically move customers down mid-billing-cycle. Credits for downward variance are issued as one-off "stepdown credits" at the end of the period if usage is below the committed floor. Those credits can be applied to the next invoice or held as a pool for burst months.

- Feature gating: Bundled features like priority routing are tied to the active tier. If you elect a downgrade mid-contract we can provide a 30-day feature grace window for critical features (priority routing, cache warm pools) as part of a negotiated concession.

- Two-year deal discount / protection: We can include a stepdown protection clause that covers up to 15% downward variance for year 1 and 10% for year 2 at the discount level you're asking. To hit 20% coverage we'd need an additional commitment on minimum throughput or a modest increase in effective price.

Details: I've attached an updated "Stepdown_Playbook.xlsx" with two example approaches:
A) Credit pool model — unused committed tokens below floor convert to credits (applied next invoice).
B) Tier-hold model — for a negotiated fee we hold the current tier and features for up to 3 months while usage stabilizes.

Logan — can you confirm any technical constraints for priority routing during a tier-hold?

Next steps: If either A or B looks workable, we can mark that in the Master Agreement and push a draft to DocuSign for signatures.

Best,
Ben

--
Ben Carter
Enterprise Sales
Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com
+1 650-555-0199

Attachment: Stepdown_Playbook.xlsx (see sheet: examples)

Date: Thu, 29 Mar 2028 10:31:00 -0700
From: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Seth Romero <seth@mindwell.io>
Cc: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>
Subject: Re: Question on packaging — stepdown rules & proration

Hi all,

Technical note on priority routing during a tier-hold: we can maintain the routing policy (same queue priority + cache warm pool) for the 30-day grace window without architectural changes, provided MindWell keeps the same model and seq-length distribution. If you change workloads (e.g., move to longer sequences or different models) we may need a short onboarding to reprofile kernels — that would be included in the tier-hold fee.

Also wanted to flag monitoring: to avoid accidental overages we recommend enabling per-route throttling and a pre-burst alert (we can add a webhook to your monitoring to notify when you hit 85% of the committed throughput).

Cheers,
Logan

--
Logan Wright
Solutions Architect, Redwood Inference
logan.wright@redwood.com

Date: Thu, 29 Mar 2028 14:07:00 -0700
From: Priya Shah <priya.shah@mindwell.io>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Seth Romero <seth@mindwell.io>
Cc: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>
Subject: Re: Question on packaging — stepdown rules & proration

Thanks Ben / Logan — quick procurement check:

- For the credit pool model can you confirm whether credits expire? Our compliance group wants a maximum 12-month life on any credits.
- For the tier-hold fee, we'd prefer any fees be amortized into the invoice rather than shown as a separate one-off line item.

If we can get credits with 12-month expiry and amortized tier-hold fees, I think Priya and I can accept the stepdown protection in principle and move to finalize the two-year commitment.

Regards,
Priya

--
Priya Shah
Sr. Manager, Procurement
MindWell Health

Cc: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>, Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwood.com>
Subject: Re: Question on packaging — stepdown rules & proration — confirmed path and next steps

Seth / Priya — thanks for the quick turn. Summary / confirmed path below: 

1) Credit pool: credits will be issued for mid-cycle downward variance and expire in 12 months. Credits may be applied to invoices or held as a pool for burst months as requested.
2) Tier-hold grace: 30-day grace window for bundled features (priority routing + cache warm pools) at no charge for the first quarter for onboarding; beyond that the tier-hold fee will be amortized monthly across invoices (we'll show an amortized line).
3) Protection coverage: we can commit to 15% protection year 1 / 10% year 2 at the pricing discussed. For 20% coverage you can either lift the committed throughput floor by ~12% or accept a modest 4% uplift on effective price — happy to run the math in the attached spreadsheet.

Action items: 
- Kimberly to update MA with credit expiry language and amortization clause (see redlines attached).

title: Chartwise POC: intermittent SDK disconnects + session affinity ask
preview:
From: Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.com>
To: Dev Patel <dev_patel@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>
Cc: Logan Wright <logan_wright@redwood.com>, Nora Patel <nora@chartwise.ai>, Samir Rao <samir@chartwise.ai>
Date: 2028-03-07T09:04:00-07:00
Subject: Chartwise POC: intermittent SDK disconnects + session affinity ask

Hi team,

Quick one from the Chartwise demo this morning — Nora and Samir saw a couple of mid-session disconnects when they were running a 30-minute guided intake demo (video attached). They reported the client SDK reconnects but some conversational context is lost (user is asked a repeat question).

Two asks:
- Can Platform/Runtime triage the root cause of the disconnects? The attached session-logs contain SDK debug output + server-side traces. Key symptoms: 1) client receives a TCP reset, 2) subsequent reconnect establishes a new session_id and KV cache isn't restored.
- Product question: Chartwise asked whether we can support sticky session behavior at the SDK layer or a server-side token that preserves KV cache across reconnects for short-lived reconnects (30s). If not product-ready, any recommended temporary workaround?

Impact: this is blocking their demo flow and they want to validate multi-user guided intake before next week. Deal is CH-2028-032; expansion opportunity if POC succeeds.

Next steps I propose: initial triage from infra, then a short call (30m) with Chartwise + us to validate a mitigation. Can Platform provide a quick hypothesis and a hotfix plan?

Thanks,
Karthik
--
Karthik Iyer
Senior Sales Engineer, Redwood Inference


From: Dev Patel <dev_patel@redwood.com>
To: Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>
Cc: Logan Wright <logan_wright@redwood.com>, Nora Patel <nora@chartwise.ai>, Samir Rao <samir@chartwise.ai>
Date: 2028-03-07T11:22:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Chartwise POC: intermittent SDK disconnects + session affinity ask

Thanks for the detailed notes and logs, Karthik. I pulled the traces you attached. Quick read: we see repeated connection resets from the client side (RST) followed by a successful reconnect that results in a new runtime session allocation. A few initial hypotheses:

1) Client network flapping causing TCP resets — we have a few RSTs with no corresponding server errors.
2) Session allocation policy in the runtime currently associates KV cache with the ephemeral runtime session id. Reconnect creates a new session id so cache isn't rehydrated.

Short-term mitigation we can try this afternoon:
- Enable SDK keepalive at client (we have a hidden knob in the Node SDK to bump keepalive interval to 15s). I can give the command to Chartwise to test.
- Patch the runtime routing to accept a reconnect token that maps to the previous KV cache for N=60s. This is a small change but needs a targeted rollout (non-breaking). ETA for a PR + canary: ~36–48 hours if we prioritize.

Questions for product: is a reconnect-token pattern acceptable as a supported behavior? It would mean we classify short reconnects as session continuation and carry KV cache for a configurable TTL. That has implications on cache memory accounting and multi-tenant semantics.

I'll attach a sanitized log snippet and a quick repro script we can ask Chartwise to run.

- Dev
--
Dev Patel
Staff Software Engineer, Runtime
Redwood Inference


From: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>
To: Dev Patel <dev_patel@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.com>
Cc: Logan Wright <logan_wright@redwood.com>, Nora Patel <nora@chartwise.ai>
Date: 2028-03-07T13:05:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Chartwise POC: intermittent SDK disconnects + session affinity ask

Appreciate the fast look, Dev. From the sales-eng side: Chartwise is strongly leaning on a smooth uninterrupted UX — their workflows assume prompt continuity and they have compliance constraints on re-asking PHI questions. If we can avoid re-prompts for short reconnects, that materially improves POC success probability.

Operational ask for product/pm: if the reconnect-token approach is feasible, can we ship a documented opt-in flag in the SDK (and server-side TTL config) that Chartwise can enable for their POC? They prefer feature toggles over bespoke patches.

Logan — can you weigh in on rollout risk and observability? We need traceability of when reconnect-token was used and a fallback policy for long reconnects.

I'll tell Chartwise we have two paths: 1) try client keepalive + temporary SDK knob today, or 2) schedule a 48h targeted runtime canary for reconnect-token. Which would Platform prefer as first step?

Thanks,
Marissa
--
Marissa Cole
Principal Sales Engineer, Redwood Inference


From: Logan Wright <logan_wright@redwood.com>
To: Dev Patel <dev_patel@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.com>
Cc: Nora Patel <nora@chartwise.ai>
Date: 2028-03-07T15:10:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Chartwise POC: intermittent SDK disconnects + session affinity ask

Couple of quick notes on rollout/observability:
- Rollout risk: reconnect-token is low-risk if implemented server-side as a best-effort mapping with TTL and clear opt-in via SDK flag. We should avoid changing default behavior until we validate.
- Telemetry: we must emit an event when a reconnect-token is consumed (reconnect.token.used), plus owner labels (deal_id, customer_id). Also emit metrics for token collisions and token expirations.
- Memory: add a small accounting counter per-tenant for active reconnect tokens to prevent unbounded memory growth. TTL default 60s seems reasonable; we can tune.

Recommendation: do the quick SDK keepalive test first (fast, reversible), and kick off an RFC today for reconnect-token with a 48h delivery target for a canary PR. Dev — can you create the RFC/PR template and assign a small runtime pod to own the canary?

Regards,
Logan
--
Logan Wright
Director, Platform Reliability


From: Dev Patel <dev_patel@redwood.com>
To: Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>, Logan Wright <logan_wright@redwood.com>
Cc: Nora Patel <nora@chartwise.ai>, Samir Rao <samir@chartwise.ai>
Date: 2028-03-08T16:23:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Chartwise POC: intermittent SDK disconnects + session affinity ask

Update for the thread and action items:

What I did:
- Sent an SDK keepalive knob and short repro script to Chartwise (Nora) — asking them to enable and run the intake again.
- Opened RFC: rfc/reconnect-token-2028-03-08 (link in related_links). Draft outlines server-side token-binding, TTL=60s default, opt-in SDK flag "preserveSessionOnReconnect": true, telemetry events, and per-tenant counters.
- Started a small canary branch (runtime/reconnect-token-canary) implementing token map + eviction. Local tests pass; needs integration testing.

Temporary workaround: ask Chartwise to add the keepalive setting in their Node SDK until we can canary the server change. If they need an immediate server-side toggle, we can do a targeted canary in us-west-prod with a single runtime shard and monitor telemetry.

Next steps / owners:
- Dev: finish canary PR and run integration tests (ETA 36h).
- Logan: add observability tickets (metrics + trace events) and review memory accounting (ETA 24h).
- Marissa/Karthik: coordinate Chartwise test and confirm whether they want opt-in canary or just the keepalive knob.

I'll post the PR link here when ready and schedule a 30m sync with Chartwise tomorrow afternoon if they want.

- Dev
--
Attachments: session-logs-20280307.txt (sanitized), chartwise-demo-video-clip.mp4 (30s clip)

From: Dev Patel <dev_patel@redwood.com>
To: Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>
Cc: Logan Wright <logan_wright@redwood.com>, Nora Patel <nora@chartwise.ai>, Samir Rao <samir@chartwise.ai>
Date: 2028-03-07T11:22:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Chartwise POC: intermittent SDK disconnects + session affinity ask

Thanks for the detailed notes and logs, Karthik. I pulled the traces you attached. Quick read: we see repeated connection resets from the client side (RST) followed by a successful reconnect that results in a new runtime session allocation. A few initial hypotheses:

1) Client network flapping causing TCP resets — we have a few RSTs with no corresponding server errors.
2) Session allocation policy in the runtime currently associates KV cache with the ephemeral runtime session id. Reconnect creates a new session id so cache isn't rehydrated.

Short-term mitigation we can try this afternoon:
- Enable SDK keepalive at client (we have a hidden knob in the Node SDK to bump keepalive interval to 15s). I can give the command to Chartwise to test.
- Patch the runtime routing to accept a reconnect token that maps to the previous KV cache for N=60s. This is a small change but needs a targeted rollout (non-breaking). ETA for a PR + canary: ~36–48 hours if we prioritize.

Questions for product: is a reconnect-token pattern acceptable as a supported behavior? It would mean we classify short reconnects as session continuation and carry KV cache for a configurable TTL. That has implications on cache memory accounting and multi-tenant semantics.

I'll attach a sanitized log snippet and a quick repro script we can ask Chartwise to run.

- Dev
--
Dev Patel
Staff Software Engineer, Runtime
Redwood Inference


From: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>
To: Dev Patel <dev_patel@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.com>
Cc: Logan Wright <logan_wright@redwood.com>, Nora Patel <nora@chartwise.ai>
Date: 2028-03-07T13:05:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Chartwise POC: intermittent SDK disconnects + session affinity ask

Appreciate the fast look, Dev. From the sales-eng side: Chartwise is strongly leaning on a smooth uninterrupted UX — their workflows assume prompt continuity and they have compliance constraints on re-asking PHI questions. If we can avoid re-prompts for short reconnects, that materially improves POC success probability.

Operational ask for product/pm: if the reconnect-token approach is feasible, can we ship a documented opt-in flag in the SDK (and server-side TTL config) that Chartwise can enable for their POC? They prefer feature toggles over bespoke patches.

Logan — can you weigh in on rollout risk and observability? We need traceability of when reconnect-token was used and a fallback policy for long reconnects.

I'll tell Chartwise we have two paths: 1) try client keepalive + temporary SDK knob today, or 2) schedule a 48h targeted runtime canary for reconnect-token. Which would Platform prefer as first step?

Thanks,
Marissa
--
Marissa Cole
Principal Sales Engineer, Redwood Inference


From: Logan Wright <logan_wright@redwood.com>
To: Dev Patel <dev_patel@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.com>
Cc: Nora Patel <nora@chartwise.ai>
Date: 2028-03-07T15:10:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Chartwise POC: intermittent SDK disconnects + session affinity ask

Couple of quick notes on rollout/observability:
- Rollout risk: reconnect-token is low-risk if implemented server-side as a best-effort mapping with TTL and clear opt-in via SDK flag. We should avoid changing default behavior until we validate.
- Telemetry: we must emit an event when a reconnect-token is consumed (reconnect.token.used), plus owner labels (deal_id, customer_id). Also emit metrics for token collisions and token expirations.
- Memory: add a small accounting counter per-tenant for active reconnect tokens to prevent unbounded memory growth. TTL default 60s seems reasonable; we can tune.

Recommendation: do the quick SDK keepalive test first (fast, reversible), and kick off an RFC today for reconnect-token with a 48h delivery target for a canary PR. Dev — can you create the RFC/PR template and assign a small runtime pod to own the canary?

Regards,
Logan
--
Logan Wright
Director, Platform Reliability


From: Dev Patel <dev_patel@redwood.com>

Cc: Nora Patel <nora@chartwise.ai>, Samir Rao <samir@chartwise.ai>
Date: 2028-03-08T16:23:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Chartwise POC: intermittent SDK disconnects + session affinity ask

Update for the thread and action items:

What I did:
- Sent an SDK keepalive knob and short repro script to Chartwise (Nora) — asking them to enable and run the intake again.
- Opened RFC: rfc/reconnect-token-2028-03-08 (link in related_links). Draft outlines server-side token-binding, TTL=60s default, opt-in SDK flag "preserveSessionOnReconnect": true, telemetry events, and per-tenant counters.
- Started a small canary branch (runtime/reconnect-token-canary) implementing token map + eviction. Local tests pass; needs integration testing.

Temporary workaround: ask Chartwise to add the keepalive setting in their Node SDK until we can canary the server change. If they need an immediate server-side toggle, we can do a targeted canary in us-west-prod with a single runtime shard and monitor telemetry.

Next steps / owners:
- Dev: finish canary PR and run integration tests (ETA 36h).
- Logan: add observability tickets (metrics + trace events) and review memory accounting (ETA 24h).
- Marissa/Karthik: coordinate Chartwise test and confirm whether they want opt-in canary or just the keepalive knob.

I'll post the PR link here when ready and schedule a 30m sync with Chartwise tomorrow afternoon if they want.

- Dev
--

title: Marketplace charge aggregation & EU VAT pass-through (Jan–Feb)
preview:
From: Sophie Tremblay <sophie.tremblay@edgescale.com>
To: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: accounts-payable@edgescale.com
Date: Fri, 03 Mar 2028 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Marketplace charge rollup & VAT pass-through (Jan–Feb)

Hi Karthik,

We're reconciling the Jan–Feb marketplace settlements and ran into an aggregation behavior on the marketplace invoice that applied EU VAT at the seller level for a subset of spot/marketplace charges. Our buyer teams are requesting VAT be passed through to their entity (buyer-side VAT) rather than be recorded as seller VAT — that impacts their reclaim and our cash collection.

Can you provide a mapping from the marketplace invoice line items back to the underlying usage SKUs and customer account IDs? The attached CSV contains what we pulled from the seller invoice and a few example invoice IDs (column: invoice_id). Specifically we need:
- invoice_id -> marketplace_line_id -> SKU -> buyer_entity_id
- confirmation whether Redwood can emit corrected invoice lines or a credit memo for the VAT amount for EU buyers

We have a pending partial payment hold on a few invoices until we confirm the correct VAT treatment (AP is copied). If you need a sample of the buyer-side tax registration details I can send that.

Attachment: [edgescale_marketplace_charges_Q1.csv (CSV, 42KB)]

Thanks,
Sophie Tremblay
Senior Finance Operations
EdgeScale Corporation


From: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwoodinference.com>
To: Sophie Tremblay <sophie.tremblay@edgescale.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, 03 Mar 2028 11:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Marketplace charge rollup & VAT pass-through (Jan–Feb)

Hi Sophie — thanks for the note and the heads-up.

We can support line-level reconciliation but need a couple of things from your side to ensure we map the seller invoice lines to our internal usage records:
- the exported invoice_id list (looks like you included a few in the CSV — confirm full list)
- a sample buyer_entity_id / tax reg number for one representative customer so we can test the pass-through flow
- whether the VAT in question is 0%/reclaimable or a charged VAT being withheld by the marketplace

Action from our side: I'll kick off a ledger trace and ask Marissa to confirm whether we can issue a corrective credit memo (or whether we should push a corrected invoice to the marketplace provider). Logan, can you take lead on ledger mapping and advise on posting/credit impact?

We'll respond with a proposed remediation plan within 48 hours. If you can re-send the CSV (full export) and a short list of affected invoice_ids that would speed this up.

Best,
Karthik Iyer
Marketplace Billing Lead
Redwood Inference

Thanks Karthik — attaching the full export. The CSV has these columns: invoice_id, marketplace_line_id, sku, gross_amount, vat_amount, buyer_entity_id, buyer_vat_number. We've highlighted the 12 invoice_ids in the "VAT_impact" tab.

We are open to either:
- Redwood issuing a correction/credit memo for the VAT element (preferred), or
- Redwood providing the corrected invoice lines so the marketplace can re-emit with buyer-side VAT.

AP is planning to make a partial settlement for non-disputed amounts; they'll hold ~€18,400 pending the VAT correction. We'll sign an amendment for the remainder if needed — Marco from our cloud broker team circled in to help with the marketplace flow. DocuSign for the amendment is here: https://www.docusign.com/recipient/ABC-edgescale-amend

Attachment: [edgescale_marketplace_charges_Q1.csv (CSV, 86KB)]

Sophie Tremblay
Senior Finance Operations
EdgeScale Corporation

Date: Mon, 05 Mar 2028 09:22:00 -0700
Subject: Internal: EdgeScale marketplace VAT split — action items

Folks — looping internal owners so we can triage and close the hold quickly. Summary of suggested approach:

1) Ledger mapping (Logan)
- Run join between edgescale_marketplace_charges_Q1.csv -> internal usage table on marketplace_line_id and sku. Create a ledger reclassification file mapping invoice_id -> redwood_account -> posting_code (VAT_pass_through / VAT_seller).
- Flag any lines where buyer_entity_id is missing.

2) Correction path (Karthik + Legal ops)
- Determine whether to issue a Redwood correction memo (credit memo) versus requesting marketplace re-emit. Credit memo preferred for speed. Karthik, capture accounting impact and GL codes.

3) Payments and collection (Vanessa)
- Confirm AP holdback amount (Sophie said ~€18,400). If we agree to credit, propose timeline for applying a one-off credit ahead of next payout cycle.

4) External comms
- Ask Sophie to confirm buyer VAT reg numbers for the 3 largest impacted buyer_entities so we can validate reclaimability.

Timeline: logan to deliver mapping by 48 hours; karthik to propose credit memo language by end of week; vanessa to confirm collection treatment and payment timing.

Attachment: [internal_recon_template.xlsx (XLSX, 18KB)]

Thanks,
Marissa Cole
Marketplace Operations Manager
Redwood Inference

We completed the mapping and confirm: 11 of the 12 highlighted invoice_ids map cleanly to marketplace_line_ids in our usage ledger. One invoice has a missing buyer_entity_id and we've asked Sophie to provide that.

Decision and next steps we can commit to:
- Redwood will issue a one-off credit memo for the total VAT amount for the affected invoices. We'll post a redwood_correction_memo_Mar2028.pdf to your portal and apply the credit in the next settlement run (expected payout cycle: 2028-03-21).
- For long-term hygiene, we'll add a marketplace_line attribute (buyer_entity_verification) and push a small schema change so future exports include buyer_vat_number on our side. That should avoid similar aggregation mismatches.
- Vanessa, please confirm collection note to AP so they can release the held €18,400 once the credit posts.

Thanks,
Logan Wright
Senior Financial Systems Analyst
Redwood Inference

title: P95 Latency Spike — Action Matrix & Customer Narrative
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
Cc: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>
Date: 2027-12-20T08:12:00-08:00
Subject: Alert: BrightCart p95 spike 07:58-08:06 PST (initial telemetry)

Team —

Monitoring fired for BrightCart: sustained p95 jump from ~140ms to ~980ms across gen-api-rt group between 07:58 and 08:06 PST. Throughput remained steady; error rate unchanged.

Quick facts: (see attached pv-p95-metrics-20271220.csv)
- Host pool: gen-api-rt (multi-tenant)
- Observed: token-processing p95 increased to 980ms for 9 minutes, then intermittent drops with secondary 45s spikes over the next hour
- KV cache hit-rate decreased by ~12 percentage points during the window

Initial triage: kernel scheduler showed a backlog on a subset of nodes (gpu-6a*, kernel queue depth spike). No pods OOM. No config rollouts in the prior 2 hours.

Immediate asks:
1) Product: confirm if any model changes or serving-config toggles were pushed for BrightCart tenant (Marissa / Karthik)
2) Infra/SRE: escalate to on-call H2 if node-level remediation needed (Logan)
3) Customer comm: we should send a one-line heads-up to BrightCart with ETA for next update (Kimberly)

I'll keep digging into kernel traces and attach a short CSV with per-node p95.

Ben
Senior SRE
Redwood Inference

---
Attachment: pv-p95-metrics-20271220.csv (CSV stub: timestamps, node, p50, p95, kv_hit_rate)

From: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
Cc: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>
Date: 2027-12-20T09:01:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Alert: BrightCart p95 spike 07:58-08:06 PST (initial telemetry)

Thanks Ben — good catch.

Summary of actions I'm asking teams to take now (action-matrix v1 attached):
- SRE (Ben / Logan): collect kernel traces for the eight affected nodes, confirm if scheduling hot-path or NVLink saturation is present; consider repro on a mirrored subset. Escalate to hardware team if traces indicate device saturation. (ETA 60m)
- Product (Marissa / Karthik): validate whether BrightCart is on any experimental model or quantization profile; check routing policy (strict routing or region pin) that might prevent fallback. If it is pinned to a variant with larger kv footprint, recommend temporary fallback to the previously stable variant. (ETA 45m)
- Customer comm (me): draft an outbound note describing impact, immediate mitigations, and next update cadence.

Two product-specific questions for roadmap/decision:
A) If we implement an automatic fallback to a compatible model-variant when kv-hit drops below threshold, what are the QoE tradeoffs? Is there an easy toggle we can enable per-tenant? (Marissa)
B) Should we prioritize an early release of per-tenant kernel scheduling limits (to prevent noisy neighbor) on gen-api-rt? Is there a small-scope patch we can backport? (Karthik)

Please update the action-matrix sheet with assigned owners and expected completion times. I'll draft the customer note and circulate for sign-off.

Kimberly Park
Director, Customer Reliability
Redwood Inference

---
Attachment: action-matrix-v1.xlsx (stub: owners, tasks, ETAs)

From: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>
To: Alex Ruiz <alex.ruiz@brightcart.com>
Cc: BrightCart SRE Team <sre@brightcart.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Date: 2027-12-20T10:05:00-08:00
Subject: Redwood update: transient higher p95 for BrightCart — immediate steps and ETA

Alex —

I wanted to make you aware of a transient latency increase we detected this morning impacting BrightCart between ~07:58–08:06 PST and intermittently afterward for a subset of requests. Key points:
- Impact: elevated p95 on generation requests (some requests saw p95 near ~1s); no increase in error rate or dropped requests.
- What we're doing now: our SRE team is collecting kernel traces and node-level metrics; product is validating routing and model variant configuration for your tenant. We have an action matrix with owners and timelines.
- Expected cadence: we will share a technical update within 60 minutes and a follow-up status note in 3 hours (or sooner if we have a remediation).

Immediate ask: if you have any live traffic ramps or batch jobs scheduled in the next 4 hours that can be deferred, please let us know — reducing request fanout helps us narrow the repro and low-impact mitigation testing.

Draft outbound note and the raw metrics CSV attached for your engineering team to review.

Thanks —
Kimberly Park
Director, Customer Reliability
Redwood Inference

---
Attachment: outbound-cust-note-draft.txt (stubbed draft)
Attachment: pv-p95-metrics-20271220.csv (same metrics)

From: Alex Ruiz <alex.ruiz@brightcart.com>
To: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>
Cc: BrightCart SRE Team <sre@brightcart.com>
Date: 2027-12-20T13:45:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Redwood update: transient higher p95 for BrightCart — immediate steps and ETA

Kimberly / team —

Thanks for the quick heads-up and the data. BrightCart can temporarily throttle our background ranking jobs for the next 3 hours and we'll notify if anything changes.

A couple of asks from our side for the follow-up:
- Please include any node IDs you suspect are noisy so we can correlate with our internal tracing.
- If there is a proposed temporary fallback variant, include expected throughput/latency delta so we can sign-off quickly.

Appreciate the fast coordination.

— Alex

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>

Date: 2027-12-20T08:12:00-08:00
Subject: Alert: BrightCart p95 spike 07:58-08:06 PST (initial telemetry)

Team —

Monitoring fired for BrightCart: sustained p95 jump from ~140ms to ~980ms across gen-api-rt group between 07:58 and 08:06 PST. Throughput remained steady; error rate unchanged.

Quick facts: (see attached pv-p95-metrics-20271220.csv)
- Host pool: gen-api-rt (multi-tenant)
- Observed: token-processing p95 increased to 980ms for 9 minutes, then intermittent drops with secondary 45s spikes over the next hour
- KV cache hit-rate decreased by ~12 percentage points during the window

Initial triage: kernel scheduler showed a backlog on a subset of nodes (gpu-6a*, kernel queue depth spike). No pods OOM. No config rollouts in the prior 2 hours.

Immediate asks:
1) Product: confirm if any model changes or serving-config toggles were pushed for BrightCart tenant (Marissa / Karthik)
2) Infra/SRE: escalate to on-call H2 if node-level remediation needed (Logan)
3) Customer comm: we should send a one-line heads-up to BrightCart with ETA for next update (Kimberly)

I'll keep digging into kernel traces and attach a short CSV with per-node p95.

Ben
Senior SRE
Redwood Inference

Date: 2027-12-20T09:01:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Alert: BrightCart p95 spike 07:58-08:06 PST (initial telemetry)

Thanks Ben — good catch.

Summary of actions I'm asking teams to take now (action-matrix v1 attached):
- SRE (Ben / Logan): collect kernel traces for the eight affected nodes, confirm if scheduling hot-path or NVLink saturation is present; consider repro on a mirrored subset. Escalate to hardware team if traces indicate device saturation. (ETA 60m)
- Product (Marissa / Karthik): validate whether BrightCart is on any experimental model or quantization profile; check routing policy (strict routing or region pin) that might prevent fallback. If it is pinned to a variant with larger kv footprint, recommend temporary fallback to the previously stable variant. (ETA 45m)
- Customer comm (me): draft an outbound note describing impact, immediate mitigations, and next update cadence.

Two product-specific questions for roadmap/decision:
A) If we implement an automatic fallback to a compatible model-variant when kv-hit drops below threshold, what are the QoE tradeoffs? Is there an easy toggle we can enable per-tenant? (Marissa)
B) Should we prioritize an early release of per-tenant kernel scheduling limits (to prevent noisy neighbor) on gen-api-rt? Is there a small-scope patch we can backport? (Karthik)

Please update the action-matrix sheet with assigned owners and expected completion times. I'll draft the customer note and circulate for sign-off.

Kimberly Park

Redwood Inference

title: Re: EU catalog VAT & MSA signer orchestration — cross-border tax mapping
preview:
From: Sophie Tremblay <sophie.tremblay@acme-eu.com>
To: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>
Cc: Jean-Luc Martin <jl.martin@acme-eu.com>
Date: Wed, 15 Dec 2027 08:12:00 +0100
Subject: Catalog tax fields + MSA redline — EU VAT handling question

Hi Kimberly,

Thanks again for the walkthrough earlier. We're trying to move the procurement portal activation forward but hit a snag on how Redwood will populate VAT handling on punchout/catelog orders that originate in our EU marketplaces. Two specific asks: 

1) Order Form: we need a clear mapping for VAT responsibilities (supplier-borne vs buyer-reverse-charge) per line item — currently our EDI and Ariba mappings expect a \"tax_indicator\" + \"vat_mode\" field. Can Redwood populate these from the catalog metadata or will we need an intermediary transform?

2) MSA redline: we attached our latest internal notes and the redline from Legal. There is a clause (Section 7.2) about cross-border tax indemnity that our tax team will not accept unless the Order Form explicitly records the VAT mode per PO line. Marissa, Mateo — looping in your legal/ops contacts here.

Attachments: Acme_MSA_Redline_v3.docx, OrderForm_EU_VAT_Spec.xlsx

Can your team propose a minimal change to the Order Form schema that keeps the MSA language intact but gives us the namespace for VAT mode? We are hoping to avoid an MSA amendment if possible — catalog metadata + orderform mapping would be ideal.

Best,
Sophie
Procurement Lead — Acme Health Systems (EU)




From: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>
To: Sophie Tremblay <sophie.tremblay@acme-eu.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 15 Dec 2027 11:03:00 +01:00
Subject: Re: Catalog tax fields + MSA redline — EU VAT handling question

Hi Sophie — thanks for the detail and the attachments. Quick notes from the ops side: 

- Redwood's standard Order Form includes an optional \"tax_mode\" attribute at the PO header level but not per-line by default. We do support per-line tax metadata in the catalog (we surface a \"vat_profile_id\" on catalog items that our runtime can carry through to PO line items).

- Short term option (no MSA amendment): we can add a per-line \"vat_mode\" passthrough on the connector that maps Acme's catalog \"VAT profile\" to the Order Form line attribute. That requires a connector mapping rule and a short onboarding task.

- Longer term: product would prefer adding \"vat_mode\" as a first-class optional Order Form line field (backwards compatible). That would require a small schema bump and rollout window (2–3 sprints).

Marissa / Mateo — Sophie highlighted Section 7.2 in their redline. Can legal confirm whether capturing \"vat_mode\" in the Order Form payload (and persisting it for audit) is acceptable from a contract perspective and avoids an MSA change? If you need the exact clause text, see attached redline.

Sophie — if you can share a sample PO XML snippet from Acme's Ariba side (we asked for the EDI sample, but an Ariba-XML example will be helpful), Mateo and I can produce a mapping spec and ETA.

Thanks,
Kimberly
Head of Deployments — Redwood Inference




From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
To: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>
Cc: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.com>, Sophie Tremblay <sophie.tremblay@acme-eu.com>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 16 Dec 2027 09:31:00 +01:00
Subject: Re: Catalog tax fields + MSA redline — legal take and recommended path

Kim/Mateo/Sophie,

I reviewed Section 7.2 redline and spoke with tax counsel. Summary: 

- We prefer not to amend the MSA if we can capture unequivocal evidence on the transactional record that shows the billed VAT treatment per PO line. If the Order Form (or the incoming Ariba payload) carries an explicit \"vat_mode\" value and we retain that in audit logs for 7 years (per our retention policy for finance), that's an acceptable operational control that satisfies the indemnity concern.

- Two conditions from Legal: 
  1) The Order Form must include an enumerated set of accepted values (e.g., \"supplier_borne\", \"reverse_charge\", \"out_of_scope\"). Free-text is not acceptable.
  2) We must include a short term fallback policy: if the \"vat_mode\" is missing, Redwood will tag the line as \"unknown_vat\" and route the PO to a human review queue (we should document SLA for escalations).

Operational impact: Logan — ops will need to create the review queue and the connector mapping. Mateo — can you draft the mapping spec (catalog field -> orderform.vat_mode) and confirm how we will persist the field in the PO history? Sophie — if Acme can populate an enumerated value from your side, we'll be able to keep this as an onboarding task rather than an MSA amendment.

Attached: legal notes (inline comments on Acme_MSA_Redline_v3.docx).

Marissa
Senior Counsel — Redwood Inference




From: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>
To: Sophie Tremblay <sophie.tremblay@acme-eu.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.com>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 17 Dec 2027 16:45:00 +01:00
Subject: Re: Catalog tax fields + MSA signer orchestration — next steps and ETA

Sophie — thanks for the fast responses and for the Ariba sample you dropped in earlier today (received and verified). Next steps and timeline: 

1) Connector mapping spec (Mateo) — ETA EOD Mon (20 Dec). Will include sample Ariba-XML -> Redwood Order Form line mapping and exact expected enum values for \"vat_mode\".

2) Ops configuration (Logan) — will provision a human review queue and set an SLA: 8 business hours for tax-unknown flags during the onboarding phase. We'll also add monitoring alerts to the activation dashboard so your procurement team can see outstanding reviews.

3) Legal controls (Marissa) — Redwood will persist order.vat_mode in audit logs for at least 7 years and include the value in our PO-level export for finance. Marissa will provide a short clause we can include in the onboarding acceptance checklist (not an MSA amendment).

4) Go/no-go decision: Sophie, if Acme can supply the enumerated vat_mode on catalog export (one of [supplier_borne, reverse_charge, out_of_scope]) we can proceed without contract changes. If not, we will schedule a brief call to discuss MSA amendment wording.

I'll kick off an internal onboarding ticket and share the mapping doc/drive folder.

Best regards,
Kimberly
Head of Deployments — Redwood Inference

--
Redwood Inference | https://redwood.inference.example | +1 (415) 555-0123
