title: Re: Sponsor optics and speaker allocation for EdgeConf 2027
preview:
From: Nate Collins <nate@brightstage.events>
To: Camila Reyes <camila_reyes@redwood.com>, Kimberly Park <kimberly_park@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 4 Jun 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: EdgeConf 2027 — proposed sponsor addendum and optics

Hi Camila / Kimberly,

Thanks again for the conversation yesterday — excited to have Redwood at EdgeConf. I attached a draft addendum (EdgeConf_sponsor_addendum_v2.pdf) that reflects the sponsor optics we discussed. High level:

- We can upgrade Redwood to top-tier logo placement on the main stage banner (center-left) if you confirm the Silver-level booth (10x10) rather than the Platinum island.
- In exchange we can offer: a 6-minute stage intro slot during the main keynote session, three 1-hour demo pod reservations (priority placement in Demo Pod A), and guaranteed speaking mention in partner announcements.
- AV & logistics: standard booth power + 3 exhibitor passes; green room access only for panelists (not keynote).

If Redwood wants the full 30-minute keynote instead of the 6-minute intro, we'd need to remove the top-tier logo upgrade or adjust fee by +$15k.

Can you confirm which path you prefer? If you want changes to the addendum, mark them and send back — we can redline and get procurement set up.

Attaching: EdgeConf_sponsor_addendum_v2.pdf, floorplan_demo_pod_A3.png

Best,
Nate Collins
Director, Partnerships — BrightStage Events
Nate Collins | brightstage.events


From: Camila Reyes <camila_reyes@redwood.com>
To: Nate Collins <nate@brightstage.events>
Cc: Kimberly Park <kimberly_park@redwood.com>, Logan Wright <logan_wright@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 4 Jun 2027 11:05:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: EdgeConf 2027 — proposed sponsor addendum and optics

Nate — thanks for the draft and the clear options. Quick responses below.

1) Keynote vs logo: Redwood's strategic priority is visibility + product demo time. We are prepared to accept a Silver 10x10 booth (we have portable kit for smaller footprints) if we can secure the full 30-minute keynote slot on Day 1 (prime afternoon). The keynote needs: 30 min, closed-door prep window, green room access for presenter + engineer, and upstream AV check 60 mins before.

2) Demo pod hours: we accept the three 1-hour slots, but need them scheduled across peak attendee flow (not all consecutive). Priority placement in Demo Pod A is non-negotiable. Can you confirm power and ethernet per pod, plus a dedicated point of contact for on-site tech issues?

3) Logo placement: top-tier placement is essential for branding — center-left position acceptable, but please confirm the size (logo to occupy at least 18% of the main stage banner area) and that our logotype will be used (not the stacked mark).

4) Fees & contract: we'll accept the +$15k fee to upgrade to full keynote, but need that written into the addendum and an explicit cancellation/force majeure clause for travel disruptions. We also ask for a 48-hour DocuSign turnaround after final addendum is agreed.

Looping in Kimberly & Logan for ops/redlines. Kimberly — can you review the attached addendum and propose redlines on AV, shipping window, and deliverable timing? Logan — can you confirm whether a 10x10 footprint change needs different power or carpeting?

If we can get a redline back by Tuesday EOD, I can approve and sign quickly.

Thanks,
Camila Reyes
Head of Events — Redwood Inference
camila_reyes@redwood.com | +1 415 555 0116


From: Kimberly Park <kimberly_park@redwood.com>
To: Camila Reyes <camila_reyes@redwood.com>, Logan Wright <logan_wright@redwood.com>
Cc: Nate Collins <nate@brightstage.events>
Date: Sat, 5 Jun 2027 09:32:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: EdgeConf 2027 — proposed sponsor addendum and optics (redlines)

Camila / Logan / Nate — thanks for the thread. I've reviewed the addendum and added a redlined version (redline_addendum_camila_kim.pdf). Key redlines and asks summarized here:

- Keynote staging & AV: require a 30-min dedicated keynote slot with a 10-min Q&A buffer. Need two wireless lav mics, a direct laptop HDMI + USB-C feed test, and an AV tech assigned for the session (on call 60 mins pre and during run). Please confirm green room capacity for 3 people.
- Demo pod tech: each pod requires 110V (x2) + single 1Gbps ethernet point. On-site tech POC and escalation number required.
- Booth & branding: confirm logo filesize and vector format deadline (we'll deliver vector by 2027-06-14). Logo sizing: 18% of banner area as Camila requested; please confirm mock.
- Shipping & storage: inbound shipping window for Redwood crates must be Day -2 to Day -1. Outbound pickup no earlier than Day +1. Provide advance access to shipping label portal or manifest.
- Force majeure: include travel disruption clause with credit option (if speakers cannot travel for COVID/air issues, allow virtual delivery with recorded session accepted as full performance).

I'll attach the redline pdf. Nate — if you can accept the redlines in principle, please send a final addendum with the +$15k fee and a DocuSign link. Ops team (Logan) — confirm that a 10x10 footprint is fine with carpeting and the standard show's power pack; if not, list additional charges.

Thanks — kim

Kimberly Park
Senior Partnerships Manager — Redwood Inference
kimberly_park@redwood.com | +1 650 555 0184


From: Nate Collins <nate@brightstage.events>
To: Camila Reyes <camila_reyes@redwood.com>
Cc: Kimberly Park <kimberly_park@redwood.com>, Logan Wright <logan_wright@redwood.com>, Marta Lopez <marta@edgeconf.com>
Date: Mon, 7 Jun 2027 17:03:00 -07:00
Subject: Final addendum & DocuSign link — EdgeConf 2027

Camila / Kimberly / Logan — thank you for the clear redlines; we've accepted them in principle. Key confirmations:

- Redwood will be upgraded to a 30-minute keynote on Day 1 (including 10-min Q&A). Green room access for 3 named personnel confirmed. AV tech assigned for the block, and a pre-show 60-min run-through scheduled.
- Demo Pod A: three 1-hour slots scheduled across Day 1 (11:00–12:00), Day 1 (15:30–16:30), Day 2 (10:30–11:30). Each pod has 2x110V power and a 1Gbps ethernet drop. On-site tech POC: Jason Miller (cell +1 312 555 0199).
- Logo placement: center-left on main stage banner; mock attached. We will accept your 18% sizing request and will accept the vector by 2027-06-14.
- Shipping window: Day -2 to Day -1 accepted. Outbound earliest Day +1.
- Fee: +$15,000 to reflect keynote upgrade.
- Force majeure: clause included allowing virtual delivery in case of travel restriction with sponsor credit option.

I've uploaded the final addendum and routed for DocuSign: https://www.docusign.com/EDGE-2027-0921-sign. Please review and sign within 48 hours to lock the slot. Attachments: EdgeConf_sponsor_addendum_v2.pdf, floorplan_demo_pod_A3.png, redline_addendum_camila_kim.pdf

Looking forward to having Redwood on the main stage.

title: Connector warmup/backoff impact on PioneerPay — temporary escape hatch request
preview:
From: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>
To: Sam Kline <sam@pioneerpay.com>
Cc: Tessa Morgan <tessa.morgan@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 17 May 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Connector warm-up/backoff behavior — field impact and temporary exemption request

Sam —

Quick note from the field after our Friday PoC run: PioneerPay is hitting sporadic 503s and ~2–4s tail latencies during the first 20s of sustained traffic when their integration spins up. The customer is using our SDK with the connector pattern (server-side warm-up requests + aggressive prefetch). We believe prefetch is thrashing the KV cache and causing transient backpressure that leads to early connection drops on our dedicated pool.

Attached are a short log excerpt and a HAR trace from the session: pioneerpay-logs-20270516.txt (2.1MB text) and diag-trace-2031.har (network trace).

Two asks from Sales Eng:
- Can Platform provide a short-lived "escape hatch" we can toggle per-route (no-prefetch / reduced-prefetch) so we can keep the customer running while we triage?
- If an escape hatch is available, what are the exact steps we should provide to PioneerPay (config snippet + expected behavior)? If not, is there a safe manual routing override we can apply for their org id?

We need to surface the impact to Product for roadmap prioritization — PioneerPay is in active procurement and this could block go-live next week. Can Platform/Runtime advise feasibility and a short mitigation plan today?

Thanks —

Jordan Blake
Sales Engineer, Redwood Inference
jordan.blake@redwood.ai
+1 415 555 0192

Attachments: pioneerpay-logs-20270516.txt (text/plain, 2.1MB); diag-trace-2031.har (har, 1.8MB)


From: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.ai>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>
Cc: Tessa Morgan <tessa.morgan@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 17 May 2027 13:45:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Connector warm-up/backoff behavior — field impact and temporary exemption request

Jordan —

Thanks for the repro and trace. Quick triage notes after a rapid look at the logs:
- The customer workload runs a prefetch loop that issues concurrent short-lived sessions to the same model variant. On our dedicated pools that pattern can cause KV cache churn and memory pressure; when memory crosses the eviction threshold we see transient 5xx responses as the runtime throttles new streams.
- There are a couple of request_ids in the log that correlate to the 503s: req=0a3f4b9d / req=0a3f4bff. If you can forward any additional IDs we can pull full traces.

Immediate mitigation we can offer (short-term):
1) Per-route override: we can create an override that sets route_prefetch: false for PioneerPay's org route. This disables the automatic prefetch warm-up for that route only. It is a config change on the control plane and reversible.
2) Backoff tweak: increase initial retry/backoff for that route to 250ms -> 1s jittered for the first 10 attempts. This reduces burst reconnects during ramp.
3) Session affinity: enable short-lived sticky sessions (30s) so their warm-up sessions reuse warmed workers and reduce KV thrash.

I can push the route override now if Sales confirms PioneerPay accepts a temporary config change. The override will be documented as: config-snippet-no-prefetch.yaml (attached). Expected behavior: immediate drop in 5xx occurrences, modest increase in initial latency (100–250ms) while warm-up is suppressed.

Longer-term fixes we should discuss with Product: make prefetch opt-in per SDK by default (or adaptive throttle) and expose telemetry on prefetch-related cache evictions.

Logistics: Jordan, if you want me to apply the override, reply-all with the org_id and I'll schedule during the quiet window (off-peak PST).

—Logan
Logan Wright
Runtime Engineering Lead, Redwood Inference
logan.wright@redwood.ai

> Sam —
> 
> Quick note from the field after our Friday PoC run: PioneerPay is hitting sporadic 503s...

Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>, contracts@pioneerpay.com
Date: Tue, 18 May 2027 10:08:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Connector warm-up/backoff behavior — field impact and temporary exemption request

Team —

Appreciate the quick triage. Two product/ops asks from Sales leadership:
- We need clear timeline/commitment from Product on when a non-invasive fix (prefetch opt-in or adaptive prefetch) is planned. PioneerPay has procurement deadlines and Legal will need an entry in the contract if we are going to rely on a temporary runtime override that changes behavior.
- Please confirm the risk profile of the override (impact to SLAs, rollback steps, telemetry to monitor). We need a one-page note we can share with the customer and our CRO.

Jordan: please include the org_id and the PioneerPay procurement contact (contracts@pioneerpay.com) if they require a formal change request.

Logan / Karthik: can we get a short ETA for a patch vs a full roadmap item? If a patch is 1–2 weeks, Sales can accept a scoped contract exception and proceed; if it's longer we need alternative escalation.

Thanks —

Tessa Morgan
Director, Sales Engineering, Redwood Inference

> On Mon, May 17, 2027 at 01:45 PM Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.ai> wrote:
> Jordan —
> 
> Thanks for the repro and trace. Quick triage notes...


From: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>
To: Tessa Morgan <tessa.morgan@redwood.ai>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.ai>
Cc: contracts@pioneerpay.com
Date: Wed, 19 May 2027 15:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Connector warm-up/backoff behavior — field impact and temporary exemption request

Tessa / team —

Thanks for flagging. Proposed plan from Product/Runtime roadmap side:

Short-term (patch, 1 week):
- Implement per-route prefetch opt-out flag on the control plane (can be flipped on/off with immediate effect). This is low-risk and we can ship as a small change to the config layer; will include a rollback path and monitoring.
- Provide the documented config snippet (config-snippet-no-prefetch.yaml) and runbook for Sales/Support.

Medium-term (8–10 weeks):
- Adaptive prefetch: runtime heuristics that throttle prefetch based on KV eviction rate and worker utilization. This will require telemetry changes and a canary rollout.
- Expose a per-route metric for prefetch-evictions in Console dashboards.

Risk and monitoring: the short-term flag is low risk. We will instrument a canary in our staging cluster and run it for 48 hours. Rollback is immediate via control-plane toggle. We will also add a temporary alert on cache-eviction-rate and 5xx-per-route.

Contracts/legal: we can accept a scoped exception for DL-2027-0937 while the short-term flag is in place; please loop Legal for formal language.

Action items I will take this week:
- Create the control-plane flag PR and target canary for Friday deploy.
- Publish the one-page risk/rollback note and attach it to the deal in HubSpot: hubspot.com/deals/DL-2027-0937 (internal link).

Jordan: please confirm PioneerPay org_id so we can create the override immediately after canary.

Karthik
Product Manager, Serving Runtime
karthik.iyer@redwood.ai

preview:
From: Amanda Li <amanda@meridianevents.com>
To: Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 14 May 2027 08:03:00 -0700
Subject: QR-tagging for swag dropboxes — West Coast roadshow

Hi Hannah,

Quick note from the venue team: for the May 28–30 roadshow we want each swag dropbox to have a unique QR code printed on the merchandise tag (not the box) so volunteers can scan items at check-in. That means we need the vendor to either pre-apply the QR on each piece during print, or deliver individually tagged items to our fulfillment partner before on-site kitting.

Can you confirm which approach Redwood is recommending? Also can you share: 1) a sample tag proof (size/bleed), 2) an estimated turnaround for pre-tagging 5 SKUs at 3,000 units each, and 3) any extra fees for serialization/QR printing?

Attaching the venue spec sheet for reference (see event_specs.pdf).

Thanks,
Amanda Li
Senior Producer, Meridian Events
Phone: (415) 555-0192

---
Attachment: event_specs.pdf (120KB)


From: Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwood.com>
To: Amanda Li <amanda@meridianevents.com>
Cc: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.com>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>, Alex Chen <alex@printforge.com>
Date: Fri, 14 May 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Re: QR-tagging for swag dropboxes — West Coast roadshow

Hi Amanda — thanks for the specs and the heads up. We recommend vendor-applied QR tags at print if the QR needs to be on the item tag (fewer touchpoints, lower risk at kitting). That said, PrintForge would need the QR mapping ahead of time to stitch into their print run. If we choose on-site tagging, kitting time and labor go up and there's a non-trivial chance of mismatch at the venue.

Alex (PrintForge) — can you confirm lead time and per-unit charge for: 1) printing QR on the tag during production, and 2) applying a small serialized hangtag post-print during kitting? Please call out setup fees if any.

Logan — can you advise on the format you need for QR mapping (CSV with SKU, serial range, QR string)? I put a starter template below — happy to iterate.

I'm attaching our art/layout for the tag (swag_qr_layout_v2.pdf) and adding the Drive link with working files. If we can get vendor confirmation by Monday (5/17) we can finalize the PO and keep the existing production window.

Thanks —
Hannah Schmitt
Field Marketing Manager | Redwood Inference
hannah.schmitt@redwood.com
---
Attachments: swag_qr_layout_v2.pdf
Related: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1a2b3c4d-redwood-swag-proof

To: Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwood.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.com>
Date: Sat, 15 May 2027 11:20:00 -0700
Subject: Re: QR-tagging for swag dropboxes — West Coast roadshow

Hannah / Lauren —

CSV template works. A few notes from ops:
- Preferred file: UTF-8 CSV, columns: SKU, ITEM_DESC, START_SERIAL, END_SERIAL, QR_URL, BATCH_ID.

- Please confirm whether serials are contiguous (e.g., 000001–003000) or random. Contiguous saves scan-time issues at check-in.
- QC: we need a 50-item sample from each SKU sent to our fulfillment team at least 7 days before ship to confirm scan reliability.

Attached a mapping template for your vendor (qr_mapping_template.csv).

Logan Wright

title: Admin event completeness matrix + retention carveouts (SOC 2 / SIG / CAIQ)
preview:
From: Natalie Green <natalie.green@acmehealth.com>
To: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.ai>
Date: Sun, 25 Apr 2027 15:12:00 -0700
Subject: Evidence request — admin event list + retention carveouts

Hi Kimberly,

We are pulling together the SOC 2 + SIG evidence pack and need a clear matrix of admin events that Redwood logs (names, field shape, PII status), the default retention windows, and any carveouts (e.g., privileged ops retained longer, sampling windows). Our auditor also wants to know export options (SFTP/ signed URL / incremental snapshots), redaction approach, and the SLA for on-demand pulls.

Can you provide that matrix and any short notes on export mechanics by Thu Apr 28 EOD? If it helps, we can accept a CSV with columns: event_name, category, persisted_fields, pii_present (yes/no), retention_days, exportable (yes/no), sampleable (yes/no).

Thanks — this will go into the evidence bundle.

—Natalie
Natalie Green
Compliance Lead, Acme Health
security@acmehealth.com


From: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.ai>
To: Natalie Green <natalie.green@acmehealth.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.ai>
Date: Sun, 25 Apr 2027 18:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Evidence request — admin event list + retention carveouts

Natalie —

Thanks for the clear template. We can provide an "admin_event_matrix.csv" with the columns you suggested. High level answers to the specific asks below; the CSV will have row-level notes for special handling.

What we log (summary):
- admin_user_create / admin_user_delete — user metadata, actor, target_user_id, ip, timestamp (PII: target_user_email flagged, redact in exports by default)
- role_change / permission_grant / permission_revoke — actor, target, scope (no free-text)
- api_key_create / api_key_revoke / kms_rotate — key_id (hashed in exports), actor, timestamp
- login_success / login_failure / mfa_challenge — username (hashed for export), ip, geo, device_fingerprint
- privilege_escalation / emergency_access — full audit trail; retention extended (see below)
- kv_cache_eviction / model_load / snapshot_flush — operational traces (no user PII)

Retention & carveouts:
- Default retention: 365 days for general admin events.
- Privileged actions & escalation events: 2 years (SLA for SOC 2 evidentiary window).
- Short-lived operational debug traces (model_load, small JVM traces): 30 days unless tagged for incident retention.
- Exports contain a scrubbed view by default; PII fields are redacted unless explicit legal/contractual need is documented.

Export options & mechanics:
- Full CSV snapshots: SFTP push (customer-provided host) or customer-requested S3 presigned URL.
- Incremental delta bundle: weekly or on-demand; delivered as gzipped JSONL with an HMAC manifest for integrity.
- On-demand SLA: standard expedited handoffs are 72 business hours for full bundles; 8 business hours for incremental/sampled extracts (if within our Dedicated capacity window).

Sampling & attestations:
- We provide manifested samples for SIG reviewers: deterministic sampling with seed so sample is reproducible.
- For signed attestation we include an HMAC + signer certificate and a checksum file.

I will attach the CSV and a short export instructions doc. Looping in Marissa and Logan for the technical export steps and WORM/archive questions.

Best,
Kimberly Park
Manager, Security & Compliance
Redwood Inference


From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
To: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.ai>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.ai>

Subject: Fwd: Admin event matrix — carveouts and additional fields

Kim / Logan — forwarding Natalie's request and Kim's answers. A few notes from engineering ops:

- Please ensure we include KV cache eviction metadata (eviction_reason, model_id, shard_id, timestamp). Auditors sometimes ask for cache eviction to show provenance of served tokens for certain legal requests.
- Add a timezone-normalized timestamp column (UTC) and an ingestion_offset_ms column so auditors can reconcile with CloudTrail-style timestamps.

Jordan — can you confirm the HMAC cert rotation cadence and whether we should bake a KMS key_id into the manifest?

Marissa

Can you provide that matrix and any short notes on export mechanics by Thu Apr 28 EOD? If it helps, we can accept a CSV with columns: event_name, category, persisted_fields, pii_present (yes/no), retention_days, exportable (yes/no), sampleable (yes/no).

Compliance Lead, Acme Health
security@acmehealth.com


From: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.ai>
To: Natalie Green <natalie.green@acmehealth.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.ai>
Date: Sun, 25 Apr 2027 18:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Evidence request — admin event list + retention carveouts

Natalie —

Thanks for the clear template. We can provide an "admin_event_matrix.csv" with the columns you suggested. High level answers to the specific asks below; the CSV will have row-level notes for special handling.

What we log (summary):
- admin_user_create / admin_user_delete — user metadata, actor, target_user_id, ip, timestamp (PII: target_user_email flagged, redact in exports by default)
- role_change / permission_grant / permission_revoke — actor, target, scope (no free-text)
- api_key_create / api_key_revoke / kms_rotate — key_id (hashed in exports), actor, timestamp
- login_success / login_failure / mfa_challenge — username (hashed for export), ip, geo, device_fingerprint
- privilege_escalation / emergency_access — full audit trail; retention extended (see below)
- kv_cache_eviction / model_load / snapshot_flush — operational traces (no user PII)

Retention & carveouts:
- Default retention: 365 days for general admin events.
- Privileged actions & escalation events: 2 years (SLA for SOC 2 evidentiary window).
- Short-lived operational debug traces (model_load, small JVM traces): 30 days unless tagged for incident retention.
- Exports contain a scrubbed view by default; PII fields are redacted unless explicit legal/contractual need is documented.

Export options & mechanics:
- Full CSV snapshots: SFTP push (customer-provided host) or customer-requested S3 presigned URL.
- Incremental delta bundle: weekly or on-demand; delivered as gzipped JSONL with an HMAC manifest for integrity.
- On-demand SLA: standard expedited handoffs are 72 business hours for full bundles; 8 business hours for incremental/sampled extracts (if within our Dedicated capacity window).

Sampling & attestations:
- We provide manifested samples for SIG reviewers: deterministic sampling with seed so sample is reproducible.
- For signed attestation we include an HMAC + signer certificate and a checksum file.

I will attach the CSV and a short export instructions doc. Looping in Marissa and Logan for the technical export steps and WORM/archive questions.

Best,
Kimberly Park
Manager, Security & Compliance
Redwood Inference

title: FinEdge onboarding — model shortlist, context needs & token traffic projection
preview:
From: Sarah Li <sarah.li@finedge.ai>
To: Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwood.com>
Cc: product@finedge.ai
Date: 2027-04-22T16:12:00Z
Subject: FinEdge onboarding — model shortlist & token traffic profile

Hi Connor,

Thanks for the kickoff call earlier — excited to get moving. I attached a small CSV with our current traffic snapshot (sample_traffic_profile.csv). A few concrete asks so we can make the implementation plan: 

- Based on what we described (RAG for support articles + short-form assistant replies), which model families do you recommend for: a) initial pilot, b) production with long-context (consolidated threads)?
- We think we'll need up to ~40k tokens of indexed context available to the model for multi-turn completions — is that realistic with Redwood Private?
- Quick estimate of token volumes (daily/monthly) and recommended compute profile for predictable latency (p50/p95 goals) would help our procurement team.

A couple of numbers from the CSV: peak concurrent sessions ~120, avg sessions/day ~12k, avg user turn count ~3 (prompt + 1 follow-up), average incoming text ~220 tokens, expected average generation ~280 tokens. Those are current prod numbers; we expect growth to 2x in 6 months.

Can you send a short implementation brief and a recommended model shortlist for our engineering team? Happy to hop on a follow-up call next week.

Thanks,
Sarah Li
Head of Product
FinEdge

---
Attachment: sample_traffic_profile.csv (text/csv, 42KB)

From: Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwood.com>
To: Sarah Li <sarah.li@finedge.ai>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica_patel@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>, Logan Wright <logan_wright@redwood.com>
Date: 2027-04-22T18:03:00Z
Subject: Re: FinEdge onboarding — model shortlist & token traffic profile

Hi Sarah — thanks for the data and for the clear asks. Putting a concise recommendation below so your engineers and procurement can review quickly. I’m copying Monica (CS) and Marissa (Solutions) to coordinate the MLP and a mutual action plan.

Quick recommendations (pilot -> production):

1) Pilot (fast, low-cost): Redwood-Instruct-7B-8k
   - Why: strong instruction-following, low inference unit cost, good for iterating on prompts and retrieval glue logic.
   - Use case: build proofs-of-concept, measure RAG latency and basic hallucination rates.

2) Production (long-context, primary): Redwood-Context-13B-32k (quantized option available)
   - Why: supports the large indexed context you described (up to ~32k tokens native). With quantized weights + caching we can keep per-token cost reasonable while preserving quality for multi-document grounding.
   - Notes: For your 40k indexed context we recommend a hybrid approach: keep the most relevant chunks in a 32k primary context and use local chunked retrieval + soft stitching for the rest. Redwood Private supports this topology.

3) Fallback / high-quality (costlier): Open-Llama-70B-16k (dedicated capacity)
   - Why: reserve for low-volume high-fidelity flows (legal/sensitive responses) or escalation routing. Higher cost and more memory-intensive.

Token volume & compute estimate (back-of-envelope):
- Baseline numbers you provided (current): 12k sessions/day * 3 turns/session = 36k turns/day
- Avg input tokens: 220 => 36k * 220 = 7.92M prompt tokens/day
- Avg generation tokens: 280 => 36k * 280 = 10.08M completion tokens/day
- Total tokens/day ~ 18M (current). Monthly ~ 540M. With 2x growth in 6 months monthly ~1.08B tokens.

Throughput & GPU pool suggestion for production (p50 target ~150–200ms token generation on interactive paths):
- Redwood-Context-13B-32k: start with a 4-node pool (each node: 80GB GPU class or equivalent) configured for continuous batching and 2x burst capacity. This setup gives headroom for prefix caching and reduces cold-start cost.
- If you need strict p95 latency <350ms for short replies, add an autoscale policy with a reserve of 1–2 hot nodes for peak windows.

Operational notes / assumptions:
- We assume embeddings for retrieval; embedding QPS will add ~20% to token compute depending on chunking strategy.
- Retention of logs/requests for quality monitoring is configurable; if you need 12+ month retention that impacts storage but not inference sizing directly.

Next steps I propose:
- Monica will share a mutual action plan and a short implementation brief (timeline, required infra changes for Private).
- We schedule a 60-min engineering huddle to finalize context architecture and canary plan.
- We'll include an estimated cost range (USD) per month once we confirm expected token mix and SLA.

I'll follow up with a compact implementation brief and a calendar invite for your engineering team.

Best,
Connor O'Brien
Senior Account Executive | Redwood Inference
connor.obrien@redwood.com | +1 415-555-0192

---
Note: rough numbers above are conservative—Monica will surface a refined sizing and a mutual action plan attachment.

From: Monica Patel <monica_patel@redwood.com>
To: Sarah Li <sarah.li@finedge.ai>, Connor O'Brien <connor_obrien@redwood.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>, Logan Wright <logan_wright@redwood.com>
Date: 2027-04-22T18:45:00Z
Subject: Re: FinEdge onboarding — model shortlist & token traffic profile

Hi Sarah — adding operational detail and a short MAP. Attached: mutual_action_plan.docx. High-level items we will own vs FinEdge:

- Redwood (owner): model rollout (canary -> A/B -> full), quantization recommendation, baseline batching config, and initial smoke tests.
- FinEdge (owner): retrieval chunking strategy, KMS/keys for Private deployment, and pre-production dataset access for evals.

Implementation brief highlights (TL;DR):
- Week 0–1: Environment prep (VPC peering, KMS), sample data transfer (s3 or secure upload), and embedding pipeline test.
- Week 2: Pilot deploy with Redwood-Instruct-7B-8k, run 3-day load and quality sweep, collect fairness & hallucination metrics.
- Week 3–4: Production deploy of Redwood-Context-13B-32k with prefix caching and chunk-level retrieval. Canary at 5% traffic -> ramp to 25% -> 100%.

Canary & rollback playbook (short):
- Metrics to watch: generation latency (p50/p95), failure rate, perplexity drift vs baseline, hallucination incidence on canned test set.
- Automatic fallback: route to a lower-context 13B quantized variant + synchronous human review queue for flagged responses.

Questions for FinEdge to confirm:
1) Do you want embeddings to be computed at write-time (precompute) or on-demand? Precompute reduces runtime latency but increases storage/refresh complexity.
2) Are there retention/compliance constraints for logs (30/90/365 days)?

I attached the MAP and will add action owners in the doc. Connor — we should include a short estimate for monthly cost ranges for the two model paths before the engineering call.

Cheers,
Monica Patel
Customer Success Manager | Redwood Inference
monica_patel@redwood.com

---

To: Monica Patel <monica_patel@redwood.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor_obrien@redwood.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>
Date: 2027-04-23T09:10:00Z
Subject: Re: FinEdge onboarding — model shortlist & token traffic profile

Thanks — the plan reads well. Quick answers:
- We prefer precompute embeddings for the first pass (storage ok) and will scope refresh windows per our document ingestion cadence (daily).
- Retention: product logs 90 days; audit logs 365 days.

Request: can you provide an order-of-magnitude monthly cost band for the two production paths (13B-32k vs 70B fallback) including expected compute + reserved capacity assumptions? Procurement will need that to start a PO. Also please propose a 2-week canary schedule we can include in the MAP.

Happy to take the engineering call on Thursday if that works for your team.

Sarah

title: Tiered reserved commit sizing & GPU price-exposure request
preview:
From: Logan Wright <logan_wright@redwood.com>
To: Rebecca Liu <rebecca@auroraai.com>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>
Date: Sun, 11 Apr 2027 10:12:00 -0700
Subject: Tiered reserved commit sizing & GPU price-exposure request

Hi Rebecca —

Following our call, attaching three sizing/term scenarios for the Aurora deal (light/moderate/heavy). The ask from your side was: flexibility on commit tenor and whether we can include a limited GPU-cost pass-through or indexation clause that protects both parties if market GPU prices swing.

Quick summary of the scenarios (attached):
- Option A (12m, conservative): smaller reserved SKUs, lower upfront, lower discount.
- Option B (18m, standard): mid SKUs, moderate upfront, tiered discount based on committed QPS.
- Option C (24m, aggressive): larger SKUs, higher upfront credit, max discount but with conditional GPU-price cap triggers.

Specific asks for Aurora:
1) Which scenario best matches procurement risk tolerance?

From: Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan_wright@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 14 Apr 2027 07:42:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Fwd: Tiered reserved commit sizing & GPU price-exposure request

Logan —

I like the structure you sketched. A few operational notes and a quick worked example below that I suggest we share with Aurora: 

Proposed conditional discount formula (Option B baseline):
- Base discount: 22% on committed token spend (applied monthly).
- Quarterly GPU index adjustment: If GPU_index_q / GPU_index_baseline > 1 + X, where X = 0.05 per 5% band, discount_adj_pp = -2.5 * floor(((GPU_index_q / GPU_index_baseline) - 1) / 0.05) capped at -10 pp.
- If index falls, apply symmetric +2.5 pp per 5% decrease (no negative floor).

Worked example (quarterly):
- Baseline index = 100. If quarter index = 112 -> +12% -> floor(12/5) = 2 bands -> discount_adj = -5 pp -> effective discount = 17%.
- If quarter index = 94 -> -6% -> floor(6/5) = 1 band -> discount_adj = +2.5 pp -> effective discount = 24.5%.

Operational fallback: allow Aurora one 14-day rebalancing window per quarter to move up to 25% of traffic to on-demand. We will not charge committed penalties during the window; instead, indexation will be applied at quarter close so the effective discount adjusts. This protects Aurora during capacity shocks and limits exposure for Redwood.

Conditions we need:
- Limit rebalancing to two non-consecutive quarters per 12-month period (to avoid chronic churn).
- Require Aurora to provide a 72-hour notice for the rebalancing window and an automated traffic-shift plan (to prevent abuse).

If Finance/Legal are comfortable, I can draft a short clause for the term sheet and Marissa can sign off. Also note impact on unit economics in the attached workbook (sheet: Sensitivity_P&L).

-Karthik


From: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan_wright@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 14 Apr 2027 09:05:00 -07:00
Subject: RE: Fwd: Tiered reserved commit sizing & GPU price-exposure request

Reviewed the formula and the operational constraints. Finance is OK to proceed on Option B with the conditional discount/indexation as drafted subject to two final items:

1) Limit the total upside discount movement to +7.5 pp (so we don't over-credit in falling markets).
2) Add a carveout: if GPU index falls >25% year-over-year we reserve the right to require a renegotiation conversation (not automatic).

With those tweaks I'm comfortable approving the structure and sending a redline-ready clause to Aurora. I'll attach the DocuSign-ready term template for the 18-month Option B and name Logan as the signatory on our side. Rebecca/Procurement — once you confirm the clauses, we'll send the DocuSign link.

Attachments: docsign_term_template.pdf

Marissa Cole

title: Pilot grace request — schema drift + third-party SDK outage
preview:
From: Maya Patel <maya@finlytics.io>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.com>
Cc: 
Date: Sat, 20 Mar 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Pilot blocked — schema drift + ID provider SDK outage

Hi Jordan,

Quick note from our eng lead — we started ingesting live traffic yesterday and hit repeated failures in the webhook consumer. The error looks like a combination of schema drift from our upstream partner and an outage in the identity vendor SDK (IDVault) that we use for token exchange.

Our data team is tied up with other releases this sprint and asked whether we can pause the pilot countdown for a couple of weeks while they resolve the schema changes and wait for IDVault to push a patch. Without the pause we're at risk of missing the demo window with our product committee.

Can Redwood provide a short pilot extension or sandbox credits while we stabilize? We really value the latency numbers we saw and want to keep momentum — this is not a lost deal, just an engineering timing issue.

I can share error logs or grant access to a staging cluster if that helps.

Thanks,
Maya Patel
Head of Platform
Finlytics
[Attachment: integration-error-sample.log (text)]

From: Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.com>
To: Maya Patel <maya@finlytics.io>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 22 Mar 2027 09:34:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Pilot blocked — schema drift + ID provider SDK outage

Hi Maya — thanks for the heads-up and for sharing the sample log. Sorry to hear you hit this during onboarding.

Short answers: we can try to help triage the logs and we have a couple of options for runway relief depending on scope: a standard 30-day pilot extension (self-service) or an escalated exception (requires leadership sign-off) for a longer window and credit top-up.

Marissa and Karthik — can you review the attached log and advise what we can do short-term (quick fixes / config toggles) and whether a 30-day extension is sufficient? If not, Maya, we can request an extended make-good from our side.

Maya — if you can grant staging access (read-only) and a short note on ETA from your eng lead, we’ll move quickly.

— Jordan
Account Exec, Redwood Inference
[jordan@redwood.com | +1 415-555-0199]

From: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>
Cc: Maya Patel <maya@finlytics.io>
Date: Tue, 23 Mar 2027 11:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Pilot blocked — Pilot triage + extension options

Thanks for the ping. I looked through the log Maya attached — the webhook payload mismatch is causing KV cache invalidations in the runtime and combined with the token-exchange failures from IDVault it's repeatedly retrying. Two quick things we can try today:

- Turn on lenient deserialization for the affected route (will accept migrated fields)
- Add a short retry-window override to avoid repeated token exchange attempts (workaround for the SDK outage)

Both are low-risk and we can push a config change to your staging cluster if you give us access.

For the commercial ask: our standard policy is a 30-day extension for integration delays. Given the third-party outage + schema drift, I'm prepared to escalate for a longer runway and a modest credit top-up. For an approval path I need
- short ETA from Finlytics engineering (expected days to patch)
- a signed pilot-extension-form (I attached a template)

If you can get me the ETA today, I will push for an escalated approval and propose 10k free token credits + 30 days initially while we assess. If the ETA is >21 days, we'll escalate for more.

Best,
Marissa Cole
Customer Success
Redwood Inference
[Attachment: pilot-extension-form.pdf (pdf)]

From: Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan@redwood.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>, Markus Klein <markus@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 25 Mar 2027 08:18:00 -0700
Subject: Fwd: Pilot blocked — schema drift + ID provider SDK outage (DEAL-7231)

Logan — forwarding a thread from Finlytics (DEAL-7231). Short summary: their staging saw schema drift from an upstream partner and IDVault (identity provider) reported an SDK outage that blocks token exchange. Their engineers estimate 4–6 weeks to fully stabilize (with vendor patch timelines uncertain).

We're at a point where the product committee demo is in early May and missing it materially reduces the likelihood of a Q2 close. They asked for a pause/extension. Marissa is comfortable offering the standard 30-day extension and 10k credits without leadership. Given the vendor outage and potential for >30 day delay, I recommend we approve a 60-day runway + 25k token credit as an escalated concession to preserve the deal. This is a mid-market account with estimated ARR of ~$180k in year one.

Asks: can we approve 60 days + 25k credits and issue an amended pilot term via DocuSign? If not, what's the counter?

Thanks — Jordan

From: Logan Wright <logan@redwood.com>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>, Markus Klein <markus@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 26 Mar 2027 14:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Fwd: Pilot blocked — schema drift + ID provider SDK outage (DEAL-7231)

Jordan — appreciate the context. I reviewed the ARR and pipeline risk. Approving a full 60-day concession + 25k credits is larger than our usual escalated exceptions without an executive discount review. I will approve a middle ground to keep momentum: 45-day extension + 15k token credit.

Conditions:
- Request Finlytics sign the pilot amendment (DocuSign) capturing the new end date and credits (legal sign-off required)
- Finance (Markus) to record the credit and cap exposure to the first contract renewal
- CS (Marissa) to provide an 'engagement plan' with milestones so we can reassess if further extension is needed

Markus — please confirm you can book the credit against DEAL-7231 and provide an accounting tag. Marissa — work with Finlytics to get the amendment signed; Jordan will coordinate the DocuSign link.

Logan Wright
Head of Sales—MidMarket
Redwood Inference

From: Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.com>
To: Maya Patel <maya@finlytics.io>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>, Logan Wright <logan@redwood.com>, Markus Klein <markus@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 29 Mar 2027 15:40:00 -0700
Subject: Approved: 45-day pilot extension + 15k credits — next steps

Maya — good news. Leadership approved a 45-day extension and 15,000 token credit for the Finlytics pilot (DEAL-7231). Attached is the DocuSign packet that updates the pilot end date and credits. Once signed we will apply the credit and push the lenient deserialization + retry-window override to your staging cluster for immediate relief.

Next steps:
1) Sign the DocuSign amendment: https://docusign.com/packet/AA123456789 (packet attached)
2) Confirm staging access for our CS team and drop the ETA from your eng lead in reply
3) Marissa will run the config change during a 30-minute maintenance window and monitor errors

Attachments included: redwood-amendment-docusign.pdf, pilot-extension-form.pdf, integration-error-sample.log

If anything changes from your side (vendor patch arrives sooner, or the ETA lengthens), loop me in and we'll reassess.

Thanks — Jordan
Account Exec, Redwood Inference
[Attachment: redwood-amendment-docusign.pdf (pdf)]

Hi Maria,

Best,
Karthik

Redwood Inference

title: FinPass SDK routing behavior / prefetch 503s — triage + rollout ask
preview:
From: Evan Riley <evan.riley@finpass.com>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
Cc: procurement@finpass.com
Date: 2027-03-21T08:12:00-07:00
Subject: Unexpected 503s during large-batch prefetch — logs attached

Hi Jordan,

Quick heads up from our engineering team: when we enabled the SDK prefetch option for longer prompts in our load test, roughly 10% of requests started returning 503 from the Redwood endpoint. The failures correlate with requests that include our routing hint header (x-route-hint: finpass-eu) and use the sdk prefetch/batched=true mode.

Attachments: finpass_request_dump.log, trace_id_abc123.json (small sample traces).

We have a demo next week and need a short-term plan: either a config we can toggle on our side or a temporary rollback on your end while you investigate. Can you confirm receipt and next steps?

Thanks,
Evan
Evan Riley
Head of Platform
FinPass


From: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
To: Evan Riley <evan.riley@finpass.com>
Cc: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>, Tessa Morgan <tessa.morgan@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Date: 2027-03-21T09:05:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Unexpected 503s during large-batch prefetch — logs attached

Hi Evan — got the logs, thanks for the sample traces. We'll triage immediately.

Internal note to FinPass: we'll do an initial check for handshake/header stripping and confirm whether prefetch batching interacts badly with route_hint propagation.

Proposed short-term options:
- We can provide a per-account toggle to disable prefetch on your tenant (no code changes on your side) within 24 hours.
- Alternatively, if the issue is isolated to requests with x-route-hint, we can advise a routing header rename as a quick mitigation while we patch.

I'll loop in Platform/RTO for technical triage and keep you posted by EOD PST. If you prefer the immediate toggle, say the word and I will schedule it.

—Jordan
Jordan Blake
Director, Sales Engineering
Redwood Inference


From: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>, Tessa Morgan <tessa.morgan@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Date: 2027-03-21T11:20:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Unexpected 503s during large-batch prefetch — logs attached

Quick triage update — I ran the sample through our replay harness and reproduced a 503 on ~12% of requests when prefetch/batched=true + x-route-hint present. Important observations:

- The 503s contain gateway-level errors (503 Service Unavailable) with downstream message "variant not found" in some traces.
- Request headers are inconsistent when the request is pulled from the prefetch queue: x-route-hint is present on initial client handshake but missing on the runtime side in failing traces.
- When we force single-request (no prefetch), no failures in 1k replayed requests.

Initial ask for Platform/Runtime (cc: Karthik): can you confirm whether our prefetch path performs header normalization or a header whitelisting step that might strip non-standard headers? If yes, can we add a short-term passthrough, or a config per-tenant to preserve route hints?

Attaching a quick screenshot of trace correlation where header drops occur.

—Logan


From: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>, Tessa Morgan <tessa.morgan@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Date: 2027-03-22T02:10:00Z
Subject: Re: Unexpected 503s during large-batch prefetch — logs attached

Thanks for the replay and the sample logs. Short summary from runtime:

Root cause hypothesis:
- The prefetch/batched executor uses an internal reconstructor that rebuilds requests before sending to the backend variant router. During reconstruction we canonicalize headers; non-standard headers (those not explicitly whitelisted) are dropped. x-route-hint is not currently whitelisted in the prefetch path. When the variant router receives a request without the route hint it can select a variant incompatible with the client's model-family selection, causing a downstream 503 or variant-mismatch error.

Repro steps I used: replay sample -> set prefetch enabled -> capture request at executor output. Confirmed x-route-hint missing in ~100% of failing cases.

Short-term remediation options (cost/impact):
- Hotfix A (fast): add x-route-hint to the prefetch whitelist and roll to stable within ~36–48 hours. Low risk, small kernel touch.
- Hotfix B (safer): introduce a per-tenant config "preserve-route-hint-in-prefetch" (API & config layer + migration). ~5 days including rollout and smoke tests.
- Workaround for FinPass: disable prefetch on their tenant (immediate); they'll see higher token cost/latency but no 503s.

Product ask / roadmap request: we should formalize router-affecting-header whitelisting and publish SDK guidance for headers that must be preserved (x-route-hint, x-customer-region). This is a small platform policy item that should live in the API docs and the SDK changelog.

I'll draft a minimal hotfix PR for Hotfix A and coordinate with release ops if you want the fast path.

—Karthik
Karthik Iyer
Senior Engineer, Runtime
Redwood Inference


From: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
To: Evan Riley <evan.riley@finpass.com>, procurement@finpass.com
Cc: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>, Tessa Morgan <tessa.morgan@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
Date: 2027-03-22T10:18:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Unexpected 503s during large-batch prefetch — logs attached

Evan — thanks for the patience and the sample traces. To summarize action items and timeline:

- Immediate (today): if you need zero-risk for the demo, we can flip prefetch off on your tenant now. This is reversible and doesn't require code changes from your side. (Action owner: Jordan — will schedule on approval.)
- Fast remediation (~36–48 hrs): Runtime will add x-route-hint to the prefetch whitelist and ship Hotfix A. (Action owner: Karthik)
- Medium-term (~5 days): Product to add per-tenant preserve-route-hint config and bake guidance into SDK docs. (Owners: Platform + Product; assign Marissa as PM contact.)

Marissa — can you own the product documentation task and the communication plan for customers who rely on non-standard routing headers? We should also add a short note to the SDK release notes and recommend the most robust header names.

Evan — which option do you prefer for the demo: immediate toggle off, or try Hotfix A and verify? If you want the toggle, say "toggle off" and I'll run it this morning.

Thanks all — will follow up with timelines once Karthik posts the PR link.

—Jordan
Jordan Blake
Director, Sales Engineering
Redwood Inference

title: Potential telemetry anomaly — legal hold & IR walkthrough
preview:
From: Alex Hills <alex.hills@clearline.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan_wright@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 8 Mar 2027 13:02:00 -07:00
Subject: odd token-volume spikes observed — can we confirm if this was customer-facing?

Hi Logan,

We saw an unusual uptick in token usage from our integration between 06:10–06:40 UTC this morning. The calls look syntactically normal but volume is ~8x baseline and are concentrated from a small set of tenant IDs. No consumer PII observed in the sample, but we wanted to raise a quick flag while we investigate.

Could you confirm whether Redwood ran any remediation scripts, deployment rollouts, or testing jobs during that window? If not, we may need your help pulling a sanitized telemetry snippet and placing a legal hold while we check for exfiltration.

Appreciate a quick reply — we’ll pause any planned escalations for 24 hours if you can share preliminary context.

—Alex
Alex Hills

From: Sana Kapoor <sana@acmeanalytics.com>
To: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>
Cc: 
Date: 2026-12-19T09:12:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Credit-release oscillation plan and invoice handoff

Hi Karthik,

Thanks for the call this morning. We want to finalize how the annual prepay will be released against milestones for the upcoming product rollout. High-level asks from Acme: 

- Staggered release tied to three delivery gates (Alpha, Beta, GA) rather than a flat monthly drawdown.
- Clear invoice cadence and net terms for each milestone (we prefer net 30 but can discuss).
- VAT/evidence we need for our finance team in EU and UK — proof of tax residency and a copy of Redwood's VAT registration if applicable.

Can you confirm you can issue three separate invoices keyed to the milestones and provide a single combined ledger entry for our treasury to reconcile? Attaching our internal SOW for reference.

Attachments: Acme_SOW_Milestones.pdf (external)

Thanks,
Sana Kapoor
Director, Finance Operations
Acme Analytics



From: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>
To: Sana Kapoor <sana@acmeanalytics.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica@redwood.com>, Logan Wright <logan@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-12-19T11:03:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Credit-release oscillation plan and invoice handoff

Hi Sana — great speaking earlier.

Summary / proposed approach: 

1) Milestone mapping: we will map the three gates to discrete invoice events. Proposed amounts are in the attached Prepay_Milestone_Table.xlsx (column B shows suggested allocation). We will label each invoice with the milestone code (ACME-ALPHA, ACME-BETA, ACME-GA) to aid reconciliation.

2) Invoice cadence & terms: Redwood can issue each invoice on milestone acceptance with Net 30. If you prefer installment dates regardless of acceptance we can do dated invoices (please confirm).

3) VAT/evidence: for EU/UK VAT purposes we will include Redwood's VAT registration and a signed declaration for place-of-supply. Kimberly (cc'd) will provide the VAT doc pack and the standard attestation our tax team uses.

4) Ledger/combined entry: we will also produce a single combined ledger CSV that sums the three invoices for your treasury runbook to reconcile against the annual prepay commitment.

Next steps I propose:
- Monica to confirm milestone amounts in the attached table by EOD Thursday.
- Kimberly to share the VAT pack and invoice template by EOD Friday.
- Logan to prepare the combined ledger CSV and a proposed GL mapping for your team.

If that sounds good I will ask Kimberly to draft the actual invoice PDFs and route them to billing@acmeanalytics.com once approved.

title: VeloxData pilot: SDK interop asks + short-term workarounds
preview:
From: Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.com>
To: platform-runtime@redwood.com, runtime-oncall@redwood.com
Cc: Logan Wright <logan@redwood.com>, Tessa Morgan <tessa@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 07 Dec 2026 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: VeloxData pilot: SDK interop asks + short-term workarounds

Team —

Quick summary from this morning's pilot with VeloxData (they're on nodejs client v1.3.2): during a 30-minute demo we saw repeated session-level disconnects followed by truncated streaming responses. Customer provided runtime logs attached (velox_session_logs.txt) and a short screen capture (demo_capture.mov).

Customer asks / asks from sales-eng: 
- Is there a short-term SDK flag or header we can expose to keep connections sticky and avoid prefix trimming? They need a workaround before next week's pilot expansion.
- Can platform / runtime confirm whether our prefix/KV cache eviction policy differs between hosted and dedicated routing for long-lived sessions? Velox suspects their long prompts are triggering an aggressive eviction.
- Roadmap ask: per-route cache TTL control and an opt-in client-side keep-alive header (field-configurable) so partners can opt out of prefix trimming for demos.

What I need from runtime by EOD: 
1) Short triage note on likely root cause (logs attached)
2) If feasible, a small SDK patch (or header) we can ship as a patch release to Velox (titled \"connection-sticky: true\")
3) If not feasible short-term, a clearly documented manual config for the customer (routing hint, header, or token) we can use as a stop-gap.

Thanks — Jordan
Jordan Blake
Senior Sales Engineer, Redwood Inference
jordan@redwood.com

Attachments: velox_session_logs.txt, demo_capture.mov


From: Logan Wright <logan@redwood.com>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.com>
Cc: platform-runtime@redwood.com, runtime-oncall@redwood.com, Tessa Morgan <tessa@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 07 Dec 2026 10:03:00 -0800
Subject: Re: VeloxData pilot: SDK interop asks + short-term workarounds

Thanks for the signal, Jordan — I pulled the logs you attached and started a quick pass. Initial observations:

- There's a pattern where the connection sees a sequence of small POSTs followed by a sudden 503/ETIMEDOUT and an immediate reconnect. That lines up with a keep-alive / idle timeout driven disconnect rather than purely a prefix eviction.
- I see several \"prefix_trim: true\" traces in the runtime logs around the same timestamp as the disconnects. That suggests the client is reaching a trimming threshold and then sending a follow-up request that our serving runtime treats as a new session (hence the truncated stream).

Short-term options we can do this afternoon: 
1) Expose a header we already accept but don't document: X-Runtime-Keepalive: 1 (we can enable honoring it in hosted for this customer). This requires a small config flip on the ingress layer and a short patch to the routing auth checks (low risk).
2) Add a client-side opt-in flag in the node SDK (v1.3.x) that sets that header automatically when enabled. SDK change should be minimal; we can cut a patch branch and provide a tarball to Velox.
3) If we want to avoid any code change, advise Velox to send a periodic noop token (every ~20s) to keep the connection active — I can draft a snippet.

Plan proposal:
- Today: enable X-Runtime-Keepalive: 1 for Velox on hosted (config change) + provide node SDK patch tarball (SDK-1128).
- This week: produce a short canary run (RINF-2345) to validate memory and KV cache behavior with the keepalive header enabled.
- Roadmap: add per-route cache TTL and a first-class SDK opt-in flag for Q1.

I'll open SDK-1128 and RINF-2345 and cc the oncall. Marcus / Tessa — can you weigh in on the canary constraints?

On Tue, Dec 7, 2026 at 09:12 AM Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.com> wrote:
> Team —
> 
> Quick summary from this morning's pilot with VeloxData (they're on nodejs client v1.3.2): during a 30-minute demo we saw repeated session-level disconnects followed by truncated streaming responses. Customer provided runtime logs attached (velox_session_logs.txt) and a short screen capture (demo_capture.mov).
> 
> ...


From: Tessa Morgan <tessa@redwood.com>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.com>, Logan Wright <logan@redwood.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 07 Dec 2026 11:18:00 -0800
Subject: Re: VeloxData pilot: SDK interop asks + short-term workarounds

Jordan / Logan —

Field note: Velox asked specifically for per-route cache TTL control because their ingestion flow includes very long system prompts (multi-document context). If we expose per-route TTL (even as an emergency \"annotations\" header) they could target only the demo route and avoid broader unit-economics impact.

Marissa — can you coordinate CS to get the exact pilot schedule? They mentioned expanding from a single-pilot to 3 teams next Wednesday. If we commit to the X-Runtime-Keepalive header and an SDK patch tarball today we need CS to confirm the pilot windows.

Marcus (quick): any memory or eviction risks if we keep longer-lived KV entries for a small cohort? If yes, list approximate CPU/memory delta so we can weigh the canary.

Tessa
Tessa Morgan
Senior Sales Engineer, Redwood Inference

On Tue, Dec 7, 2026 at 10:03 AM Logan Wright <logan@redwood.com> wrote:
> Thanks for the signal, Jordan — I pulled the logs you attached and started a quick pass.
> 
> ...


From: Emilia Grant <emilia@veloxdata.ai>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.com>
Cc: Nathan Cole <nathan.cole@veloxdata.ai>, procurement@veloxdata.ai
Date: Tue, 07 Dec 2026 13:05:00 -0800
Subject: Re: VeloxData pilot: SDK interop asks + short-term workarounds

Hi Jordan / team —

Thanks for the quick triage. A few notes from our side:
- Confirmed: using nodejs client 1.3.2 running on AWS Fargate; we observed the disconnect pattern when a multi-document context (~22k tokens) was loaded and then followed by a streaming query.
- Error snippet attached (velox_error_sample.json). The stack shows repeated socket resets and then a partial event stream.

We appreciate a header-based workaround and would prefer an SDK patch to keep things reproducible across demos. Pilot expansion is next Wednesday (2026-12-14). If you can provide the tarball and recommended config by EOD PST today, we can validate tomorrow.

Thanks — Emilia
Emilia Grant
Head of Integrations, VeloxData
emilia@veloxdata.ai

Attachments: velox_error_sample.json


From: Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.com>
To: Emilia Grant <emilia@veloxdata.ai>, Nathan Cole <nathan.cole@veloxdata.ai>
Cc: Logan Wright <logan@redwood.com>, Tessa Morgan <tessa@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>, platform-runtime@redwood.com
Date: Tue, 07 Dec 2026 17:03:00 -0800
Subject: Re: VeloxData pilot: SDK interop asks + short-term workarounds

Emilia — appreciate the detail and the sample. Action items and owners: 

- PLATFORM / RUNTIME (Logan / runtime-oncall): enable honoring X-Runtime-Keepalive for Velox on hosted and run a small canary (RINF-2345). Please post memory/latency metrics by 10:30pm PST.
- SDK (Logan / Marcus): cut nodejs patch tarball (SDK-1128) that sets X-Runtime-Keepalive when client option stickyConnection: true is passed. Provide tarball + install instructions to Velox by EOD.
- CS (Marissa): confirm pilot windows and coordinate validation steps with Velox; share telemetry access for the canary.
- SALES ENG (Tessa / Jordan): draft an ops note and a short runbook for the customer fallback (periodic noop) if we see further disconnects.

Related links: HubSpot deal (https://app.hubspot.com/deal/67890123), Jira tickets SDK-1128 and RINF-2345.

I'll follow up if anything changes and will hold the release notes for the tarball brief. Thanks everybody — we really want this to be a smooth pilot move to expansion next week.

Best,
Jordan Blake
Senior Sales Engineer, Redwood Inference
jordan@redwood.com

Thanks for the signal, Jordan — I pulled the logs you attached and started a quick pass. Initial observations:

- There's a pattern where the connection sees a sequence of small POSTs followed by a sudden 503/ETIMEDOUT and an immediate reconnect. That lines up with a keep-alive / idle timeout driven disconnect rather than purely a prefix eviction.
- I see several \"prefix_trim: true\" traces in the runtime logs around the same timestamp as the disconnects. That suggests the client is reaching a trimming threshold and then sending a follow-up request that our serving runtime treats as a new session (hence the truncated stream).

Short-term options we can do this afternoon: 
1) Expose a header we already accept but don't document: X-Runtime-Keepalive: 1 (we can enable honoring it in hosted for this customer). This requires a small config flip on the ingress layer and a short patch to the routing auth checks (low risk).
2) Add a client-side opt-in flag in the node SDK (v1.3.x) that sets that header automatically when enabled. SDK change should be minimal; we can cut a patch branch and provide a tarball to Velox.
3) If we want to avoid any code change, advise Velox to send a periodic noop token (every ~20s) to keep the connection active — I can draft a snippet.

Plan proposal:
- Today: enable X-Runtime-Keepalive: 1 for Velox on hosted (config change) + provide node SDK patch tarball (SDK-1128).
- This week: produce a short canary run (RINF-2345) to validate memory and KV cache behavior with the keepalive header enabled.
- Roadmap: add per-route cache TTL and a first-class SDK opt-in flag for Q1.

I'll open SDK-1128 and RINF-2345 and cc the oncall. Marcus / Tessa — can you weigh in on the canary constraints?
